title: Can employer deduct employer's share from wages?
content: Q: Can employer deduct employer's share from wages? A: No. An employer cannot deduct the employer's share of PF contribution from the wages of employees. Any such deduction is a criminal offence under the EPF & MP Act, 1952.

title: Can wages be reduced on account of EPF payment?
content: Q: Can wages be reduced on account of EPF payment? A: No. Reducing wages on account of EPF payment is specifically barred under section 12 of the EPF & MP Act, 1952.

title: Can daily rated or piece rated employee become EPF member?
content: Q: Can daily rated or piece rated employee become EPF member? A: Yes. A daily rated employee or piece rated employee can become a member of the EPF.

title: How is contribution determined for daily/piece rate employees?
content: Q: How is contribution determined for daily/piece rate employees? A: The wages paid in a calendar month will be taken to determine the contribution due for daily or piece rate employees.

title: Is member entitled to full interest on belated deposit by employer?
content: Q: Is member entitled to full interest on belated deposit by employer? A: Yes. After realising dues, PF members will be given full interest for each due month. The employer shall be charged penal interest under section 7Q and penal damages under section 14B of the Act.

title: Is PF contribution payable on subsistence allowance during suspension?
content: Q: Is PF contribution payable on subsistence allowance during suspension? A: No. PF contribution is not payable on subsistence allowance paid during the period of suspension.

title: Can employee contribute to EPF after leaving service?
content: Q: Can employee contribute to EPF after leaving service? A: No. In the absence of wages and an employer, no contribution can be recovered. Any contribution by the member must be matched with the employer's share.

title: Employer deducted PF but did not pay EPFO. What is the remedy?
content: Q: Employer deducted PF but did not pay EPFO. What is the remedy? A: EPFO will invoke penal provisions of the Act to recover dues from the employer. Complaints can be lodged with Police under section 406/409 of IPC by EPFO for criminal action against defaulting employers.

title: What is the effect of non-payment of PF dues by employer?
content: Q: What is the effect of non-payment of PF dues by employer? A: The Provident Fund amount due to the member will be paid only to the extent of the amount realised from the employer. Non-payment by employer directly impacts member's PF settlement.

title: Can employer recover outstanding dues from PF amount payable to member?
content: Q: Can employer recover outstanding dues from PF amount payable to member? A: No. An employer cannot recover any outstanding dues from the PF amount payable to a member. This is totally prohibited under the EPF & MP Act, 1952.

title: What measures are used to recover PF from a defaulting employer?
content: Q: What measures are used to recover PF from a defaulting employer? A: Measures include: Attachment of Bank Accounts, Realisation of dues from Debtors, Attachment and Sale of properties, Arrest and Detention of the Employer, Action under Section 406/409 IPC and Section 110 CrPC, Prosecution under section 14 of EPF & MP Act 1952.

title: How is member informed about non-payment of contributions by employer?
content: Q: How is member informed about non-payment of contributions by employer? A: The Annual PF Statement of Account/Member Passbook indicates the amount paid by the employer. If member has activated UAN, non-payment can be verified monthly via e-passbook. Members also receive SMS on registered mobile on credit of monthly contribution.

title: Can PF amount credited to member account be attached against liability?
content: Q: Can PF amount credited to member account be attached against liability? A: No. The Provident Fund enjoys protection against attachment by any Court as per section 10 of the EPF & MP Act, 1952.

title: When employer is insolvent or company wound up, are PF contributions priority debts?
content: Q: When employer is insolvent or company wound up, are PF contributions priority debts? A: Yes. When an employer becomes insolvent or a company is wound up, PF contributions are paid in priority over other debts.

title: If member does not collect wages, can PF still be deducted?
content: Q: If member does not collect wages, can PF still be deducted? A: Yes. The employer, before paying the member wages, is required to deduct PF contribution from wages and pay to the Regional PF Commissioner. PF can be deducted even if wages are not collected.

title: Can member pay contribution in excess of 12% statutory rate?
content: Q: Can member pay contribution in excess of 12% statutory rate? A: Yes. A member can pay voluntary contribution above the normal 12% of Rs.15,000. Total contribution (voluntary + mandatory) can be up to Rs.15,000 per month. The employer may restrict his own share to the statutory rate. Member can also contribute on higher wages above Rs.15,000 after getting permission from APFC/RPFC under para 26(6) of the Scheme.

title: Can member demand to see recovery of contributions from employer?
content: Q: Can member demand to see recovery of contributions from employer? A: Yes. The contribution card of each member in Form 3-A/ECR copy can be demanded from the employer.

title: How are contract employees' PF protected when contractor defaults?
content: Q: How are contract employees' PF protected when contractor defaults? A: It is the duty of the principal employer to ensure the contractor discharges liability. The Principal Employer must ensure all eligible contract employees are enrolled and complied every month. Principal Employer can check remittance using the Establishment Search on www.epfindia.gov.in. Ensuring all contract employees activate UAN helps prevent defaults.

title: Can member refuse to contribute to the Pension Fund?
content: Q: Can member refuse to contribute to the Pension Fund? A: No. The Pension contribution is only a diversion from the employer's share of Provident Fund. Hence no consent is required from the member and refusal does not arise.

title: Can employer stop paying EPF for member aged 55 or 60?
content: Q: Can employer stop paying EPF for member aged 55 or 60? A: No. EPF contributions must be paid until the date the employee leaves service, irrespective of age. Employees who cease to be EPS (pension) members will get the employer's 8.33% contribution in PF instead.

title: PF not settled in 20 days. Who to approach?
content: Q: PF not settled in 20 days. Who to approach? A: The member can approach the Regional PF Commissioner in charge of grievances, file a complaint on the EPFiGMS website (http://epfigms.gov.in/) under FOR EMPLOYEES section, or appear before the Commissioner in the 'Nidhi Apke Nikat' program conducted on the 10th of every month.

title: Is there a time limit for withdrawal of Provident Fund?
content: Q: Is there a time limit for withdrawal of Provident Fund? A: Only in case of resignation from service (not superannuation) must a member wait for a period of two months for withdrawal of the PF amount.

title: Employer not attesting claim form. How to submit withdrawal application?
content: Q: Employer not attesting claim form. How to submit withdrawal application? A: In case of dispute, the member may get attestation from the bank where they maintain their account and submit to Regional PF Commissioner explaining reasons for not obtaining employer signature. If the member has activated UAN and linked bank account and Aadhaar, they can submit composite claim (Aadhaar) which only requires the member's signature.

title: Can PF account be transferred on change of employment?
content: Q: Can PF account be transferred on change of employment? A: Yes. On change in employment, the member should get PF account transferred to the new establishment by submitting Form 13(R). Transfer claim can also be submitted online using member interface at the unified portal.

title: If past accumulations are not transferred on cancellation of exemption, how is PF paid?
content: Q: If past accumulations are not transferred on cancellation of exemption, how is PF paid? A: The local RPFC will ensure transfer of securities/cash and arrange for refund of dues to members.

title: How is a PF member informed of transfer effected?
content: Q: How is a PF member informed of transfer effected? A: A copy of Transfer Certificate (Annexure-K) issued to the transferee Regional PF Commissioner/PF Trust giving full details of the transfer can be requested from the EPF office.

title: What is the method of crediting interest to PF subscribers?
content: Q: What is the method of crediting interest to PF subscribers? A: Compound interest is credited on monthly running balance basis at the statutory rate declared for each year. The interest is declared annually by the Central Government.

title: Does PF provide refundable loans for housing?
content: Q: Does PF provide refundable loans for housing? A: No refundable loans are available. However, non-refundable advances for housing purposes are available under Para 68-B and related provisions of the EPF Scheme.

title: Employee in non-covered establishment. Can they become EPF member?
content: Q: Employee in non-covered establishment. Can they become EPF member? A: An employee can become an EPF member only after the Act is applied to the establishment.

title: If employee is not given PF membership, who to approach?
content: Q: If employee is not given PF membership, who to approach? A: The employee can approach the employer first, and if that fails, approach the Regional Provident Fund Commissioner of the nearest PF office.

title: Employee in branch unit outside the state. Are they eligible for EPF?
content: Q: Employee in branch unit outside the state. Are they eligible for EPF? A: Yes. The Act applies to an establishment as a whole. All employees, irrespective of their place of work or location, are eligible to become members.

title: Employee working in more than one establishment. How is membership regulated?
content: Q: Employee working in more than one establishment. How is membership regulated? A: Membership is reckoned separately for each establishment under different Provident Fund Account Numbers/Member IDs.

title: Is there any age restriction for becoming an EPF member?
content: Q: Is there any age restriction for becoming an EPF member? A: There is no age restriction for becoming a Provident Fund member. However, an employee who has already attained the age of 58 cannot become a member of the Pension Fund.

title: Is there a salary restriction to become EPF member?
content: Q: Is there a salary restriction to become EPF member? A: Employees drawing basic wages and dearness allowance up to Rs.15,000 are eligible to become members. They continue as members even if pay exceeds Rs.15,000. Employees earning more than Rs.15,000 can join EPF by giving option under Para 26(6) within 6 months of joining.

title: Can an apprentice become an EPF member?
content: Q: Can an apprentice become an EPF member? A: No. An apprentice cannot become an EPF member. But when they cease to be an apprentice they should be enrolled immediately.

title: Employee earning more than Rs.15,000. Must they join EPF?
content: Q: Employee earning more than Rs.15,000. Must they join EPF? A: Such an employee is not required to become a member if not already holding PF membership. If both employer and employee are willing, membership is available by giving option under Para 26(6) of the PF Scheme within 6 months of joining.

title: Employee transferred from one establishment to another. Must they enroll again?
content: Q: Employee transferred from one establishment to another. Must they enroll again? A: Yes. They must enroll as a member under the new establishment to transfer the Provident Fund from the previous account.

title: Person working without wages. Can they get PF membership?
content: Q: Person working without wages. Can they get PF membership? A: Membership is allowed only where wages are payable to an employee.

title: Can employee become member of Pension Scheme only without contributing to PF?
content: Q: Can employee become member of Pension Scheme only without contributing to PF? A: No. Only by virtue of PF membership can one become a member of the Pension Scheme. From 01/09/2014, new employees drawing basic wage above Rs.15,000 per month can only become PF members by submitting option under Para 26(6). However, they cannot get Pension Fund membership. Both employee 12% and employer 12% shall go into PF only.

title: Can employee continue as PF member after retirement?
content: Q: Can employee continue as PF member after retirement? A: Yes. If one continues to work even after attaining the superannuation age, membership continues.

title: Is there any option for employee whether to become EPF member or not?
content: Q: Is there any option for employee whether to become EPF member or not? A: No option exists for mandatory coverage employees. All eligible employees in covered establishments are required to become members.

title: Can EPF member discontinue membership while in employment?
content: Q: Can EPF member discontinue membership while in employment? A: Not permissible. Once a member, contributions must continue throughout the employment.

title: Can employee in establishment with own PF Trust continue in private PF without joining EPF?
content: Q: Can employee in establishment with own PF Trust continue in private PF without joining EPF? A: Yes. Employee can join the private PF Trust but the Trust must take exemption from the EPF Scheme. The employee continues to be governed by the Pension and EDLI Schemes.

title: How long can employee continue EPF membership?
content: Q: How long can employee continue EPF membership? A: A member can continue EPF membership until they withdraw the amount under paragraph 69 of the EPF Scheme.

title: How is non-employment period between two spells treated?
content: Q: How is non-employment period between two spells treated? A: Non-employment period does not affect EPF but affects calculation of service to decide the quantum of benefit under the Employees' Pension Scheme.

title: What happens to EPF during closure, lockout, or strike?
content: Q: What happens to EPF during closure, lockout, or strike? A: During such period the membership continues. In the absence of wages, no recovery of contribution will be made.

title: Can employer also join the PF?
content: Q: Can employer also join the PF? A: No. Employers cannot become PF members.

title: Can employee continue EPF membership after leaving employment?
content: Q: Can employee continue EPF membership after leaving employment? A: Yes. Membership continues after leaving employment until the amount is withdrawn.

title: Can any employee join EPF directly?
content: Q: Can any employee join EPF directly? A: No. Membership is only through employment in an establishment covered under the EPF & MP Act, 1952.

title: Security Guard working for different establishments. Under whom to secure membership?
content: Q: Security Guard working for different establishments. Under whom to secure membership? A: If the employer of the Security Guard is covered under the Act, membership will be given through that employer, irrespective of the place of work.

title: If establishment has fewer than 20 persons, can employee join EPF?
content: Q: If establishment has fewer than 20 persons, can employee join EPF? A: Yes. The majority of employees and the employer can voluntarily opt for joining the Scheme under Section 1(4) of the Act (Voluntary Coverage).

title: What other benefits accrue on joining EPF?
content: Q: What other benefits accrue on joining EPF? A: On joining EPF, the member gets benefits under Pension Scheme (restricted to employees with Rs.15,000 or less monthly wage) and Employees' Deposit Linked Insurance (EDLI) Scheme.

title: Employee already drawing EPS pension. Must they join PF and Pension Fund?
content: Q: Employee already drawing EPS pension. Must they join PF and Pension Fund? A: They are required to join PF only; they cannot become a member of the Pension Scheme again.

title: Employee joining at age 58. Eligible for Pension Fund?
content: Q: Employee joining at age 58. Eligible for Pension Fund? A: No. An employee who joins an establishment at age 58 is not eligible to become a member of the Pension Fund.

title: How long can member retain PF in account?
content: Q: How long can member retain PF in account? A: Membership is retained until withdrawal of PF dues. However, if the account does not receive contributions for more than 3 years, interest will not be credited after the 3rd year.

title: In absence of nomination, how is PF amount of deceased member paid?
content: Q: In absence of nomination, how is PF amount of deceased member paid? A: It is payable to family members in equal shares under Para 70(ii) of EPF Scheme, 1952. If no eligible family member, it is payable to legally entitled persons.

title: What is the need for giving nomination for pension?
content: Q: What is the need for giving nomination for pension? A: On the death of a Pension member (before receiving pension), if there is no eligible family member, pension is payable to the nominee.

title: In absence of valid nomination and no family, who gets Pension amount?
content: Q: In absence of valid nomination and no family, who gets Pension amount? A: It is payable to the dependent parents (dependent father followed by dependent mother).

title: Can an unmarried person nominate someone from outside family?
content: Q: Can an unmarried person nominate someone from outside family? A: Yes. But on acquiring a 'Family', the nomination is treated as invalid. Benefits under EPS-1995 shall then be paid to the spouse and children, if any.

title: Can employee who did not opt for Family Pension Scheme 1971 join EPS-1995?
content: Q: Can employee who did not opt for Family Pension Scheme 1971 join EPS-1995? A: Yes.

title: What is the formula for calculating Pension amount?
content: Q: What is the formula for calculating Pension amount? A: Pension = (Pensionable Salary × Pensionable Service) / 70. Where Pensionable Salary is the average of last 60 months of salary.

title: What is the quantum of pension on superannuation?
content: Q: What is the quantum of pension on superannuation? A: A member who joins EPS-1995 at age 23 and superannuates at 58, contributing on wage ceiling of Rs.15,000 with 35 years service may get approximately Rs.7,500 as monthly pension. Formula: (15000 × 35) / 70 = 7500.

title: How is average salary determined for pension?
content: Q: How is average salary determined for pension? A: Average salary is determined for the purpose of pension to the member. It is the average of last 60 months. Non-contributory periods, if any, are reduced.

title: What are the advantages of a Scheme Certificate?
content: Q: What are the advantages of a Scheme Certificate? A: 1. It facilitates transfer of Pension Accounts when employment changes. 2. If the holder of Scheme Certificate dies, the family will get family pension.

title: Is family pension payable in case of death of member who was an Ex-serviceman?
content: Q: Is family pension payable in case of death of member who was an Ex-serviceman? A: Family pension is payable in addition to the Military Pension under Rule 54 of the CCS (Pension) Rules, 1972. Effective from 27-07-2001 only.

title: Can member seek exemption from Pension Scheme?
content: Q: Can member seek exemption from Pension Scheme? A: Individual members cannot seek exemption from the Pension Scheme. Only an establishment can seek exemption.

title: At what age is member eligible for pension?
content: Q: At what age is member eligible for pension? A: A member is eligible for pension on superannuation at age 58. If a member leaves employment between 50 and 57 years, they can avail early (reduced) pension.

title: What service is required for pension in case of death of member?
content: Q: What service is required for pension in case of death of member? A: In case of death of a member, family pension and children pension is payable even after receiving one month's contribution (including part of the month) for Pension Fund.

title: If member dies, to whom is pension payable?
content: Q: If member dies, to whom is pension payable? A: On death of the member, pension is automatically payable to the spouse (widow/widower). Children are also eligible until age 25 (2 at a time). Any disabled child gets disabled pension for life apart from the two child pensions.

title: If employer fails to pay pension contribution, is pension still payable?
content: Q: If employer fails to pay pension contribution, is pension still payable? A: Yes. Non-payment of pension contribution by employer does not affect the grant of Pension. Pension is guaranteed by the Government.

title: Can pensioner get pension anywhere in the country?
content: Q: Can pensioner get pension anywhere in the country? A: Yes. Pension can be drawn anywhere in India through the designated pension disbursing banks.

title: How is pension determined for member who worked in different establishments?
content: Q: How is pension determined for member who worked in different establishments? A: The wages and service of the member are consolidated to determine the Pension.

title: Is there any annual increase in pension amount?
content: Q: Is there any annual increase in pension amount? A: No. Once sanctioned, pension amount does not increase annually.

title: Can reduced pension be upgraded to full pension on attaining 58?
content: Q: Can reduced pension be upgraded to full pension on attaining 58? A: No. Once Pension is sanctioned it cannot be altered.

title: What are the criteria for determining date of eligibility for early pension?
content: Q: What are the criteria for determining date of eligibility for early pension? A: The member must indicate their option regarding the date for early pension in the application form. If no date is given, the date of application is taken as the opted date.

title: Can member avail pension while still in service?
content: Q: Can member avail pension while still in service? A: A member who continues in service after 58 years can avail Pension from age 58. A pensioner who has availed early pension and takes up employment thereafter will not be eligible to join the Pension Scheme. The employer's 8.33% contribution goes to EPF fund in such case.

title: Can I surrender my full pension for lump sum payment?
content: Q: Can I surrender my full pension for lump sum payment? A: No. Full pension cannot be surrendered or sold for a lump sum payment.

title: Must pension benefit be withdrawn along with PF amount?
content: Q: Must pension benefit be withdrawn along with PF amount? A: No. A member can withdraw PF amount (member share only) and maintain a lien in the Pension Scheme by availing a Scheme Certificate.

title: Can I change my Date of Birth?
content: Q: Can I change my Date of Birth? A: Yes. Date of Birth can be changed with proper documentary evidence. Refer to circular 'Change of Date of Birth for EPF & EPS members' dated 03/04/2020 on the EPFO website.

title: Can married daughter be excluded from family pension?
content: Q: Can married daughter be excluded from family pension? A: Marital status has no relevance if children are below 25 years. Eligible for family pension in the event of demise of the member.

title: Member has two wives. To whom is family pension payable?
content: Q: Member has two wives. To whom is family pension payable? A: If the second marriage is legally valid, it is payable to the eldest wife with reference to the date of marriage. On her death, payable to the next surviving widow.

title: Can pensioner nominate anyone to receive family pension in absence of family?
content: Q: Can pensioner nominate anyone to receive family pension in absence of family? A: No. In absence of family member on the date of death of the member (before eligibility for member pension), family pension is payable to nominee. Once pension is received by the member, there is no validity for nomination. A pensioner cannot nominate any person.

title: What is the effect of unemployment period under Pension Scheme?
content: Q: What is the effect of unemployment period under Pension Scheme? A: The unemployment period will be excluded from actual service. Pension is based on contributory service only.

title: Can I exclude my spouse from family pension?
content: Q: Can I exclude my spouse from family pension? A: No. The spouse is an automatic beneficiary unless legally divorced.

title: In absence of family members and nominee, to whom is pension payable?
content: Q: In absence of family members and nominee, to whom is pension payable? A: It is payable to the dependent parents.

title: Who is eligible for disablement pension?
content: Q: Who is eligible for disablement pension? A: EPS-95 member irrespective of age and service who is permanently and totally disabled during employment shall be entitled to Pension. The member must undergo a medical examination prescribed by the Central Board.

title: Why does pensioner's widow get lesser pension than non-pensioner's widow?
content: Q: Why does pensioner's widow get lesser pension than non-pensioner's widow? A: Pension is a need-based social security benefit. A pensioner's widow gets 50% of pension as the liability is restricted to one person after the pensioner attains 58. For a non-pensioner's widow, the quantum is higher considering pre-mature death and supporting children.

title: Is family pension payable to widow who married pensioner after his superannuation?
content: Q: Is family pension payable to widow who married pensioner after his superannuation? A: Yes. The widow of a pensioner is eligible for family pension irrespective of the date of marriage.

title: If widow/widower remarries, to whom is family pension payable?
content: Q: If widow/widower remarries, to whom is family pension payable? A: On remarriage, pension to widow/widower stops. Children pension is converted to orphan pension at 75% of the widow pension.

title: Till when is pension payable to widow or widower?
content: Q: Till when is pension payable to widow or widower? A: Pension is paid till remarriage of the widow/widower or till death.

title: Member has children from two wives. How is children pension determined?
content: Q: Member has children from two wives. How is children pension determined? A: Children of both wives are arranged in order of date of birth. Children pension is allowed to the eldest two children below 25 years of age.

title: Must a separate bank account be opened to draw child pension?
content: Q: Must a separate bank account be opened to draw child pension? A: Yes. A separate bank account is required to draw child pension.

title: Can widow and children draw pension from different banks?
content: Q: Can widow and children draw pension from different banks? A: No. Pension should be drawn by widow and children in the same bank and branch.

title: Who is eligible to get a Scheme Certificate?
content: Q: Who is eligible to get a Scheme Certificate? A: A member with service of 10 years or more and not attained age of 58 will be mandatorily issued a Scheme Certificate. A member with less than 10 years service can avail a Scheme Certificate to carry forward pension service (not mandatory).

title: Can member or family avail more than one pension for one service under EPS-1995?
content: Q: Can member or family avail more than one pension for one service under EPS-1995? A: No.

title: When and to whom must pensioner give life and non-remarriage certificate?
content: Q: When and to whom must pensioner give life and non-remarriage certificate? A: All pensioners are required to give a Life/Non-Remarriage Certificate every 12 months from the date pension was sanctioned or from last Life Certificate submission, duly attested by Bank Manager/Gazetted Officer. Physical certificate goes to the disbursing bank. Digital Life Certificate (DLC) is available via Aadhaar at CSCs, pension disbursing banks, Post Offices, and PF offices.

title: Can Scheme Certificate holder with 8 years service avail withdrawal benefit?
content: Q: Can Scheme Certificate holder with 8 years service avail withdrawal benefit? A: Yes. But it is advisable to complete 10 years pensionable service to be eligible for pension under EPS 1995.

title: Are orphan children eligible for double orphan pension if both parents contributed to EPS?
content: Q: Are orphan children eligible for double orphan pension if both parents contributed to EPS? A: Yes. If both parents were EPS members who contributed independently, the orphan is eligible for two pensions separately. The normal ceiling per the Employees' Pension Scheme shall continue to apply.

title: Is withdrawal benefit payable in case of defaulting establishment?
content: Q: Is withdrawal benefit payable in case of defaulting establishment? A: Withdrawal benefit will not be paid for the default period. The member is entitled to withdrawal benefits only for periods where contributions were received.

title: Can member delay pension beyond 58 years?
content: Q: Can member delay pension beyond 58 years? A: Yes. Option 1: Member can opt for pension from age 59 or 60, stopping contributions at 58, with pension increased 4% per year beyond 58. Option 2: Member can opt for pension from 59 or 60 while continuing contributions beyond 58, resulting in even higher pension.

title: Is EDLI benefit payable for death occurring after leaving service?
content: Q: Is EDLI benefit payable for death occurring after leaving service? A: No. EDLI assurance benefit is admissible only in case of death while in service.

title: To whom is EDLI benefit payable?
content: Q: To whom is EDLI benefit payable? A: EDLI benefit is payable to the persons eligible to receive the EPF dues.

title: Is assurance benefit payable to missing EPF member?
content: Q: Is assurance benefit payable to missing EPF member? A: No. Payment of Assurance Benefit under EDLI Scheme 1976 is only available on the member's death while in service to nominees/legal heirs.

title: What is the maximum quantum of Assurance benefit?
content: Q: What is the maximum quantum of Assurance benefit? A: Currently, the maximum assurance benefit under EDLI Scheme is Rs.6,00,000 (Rupees Six Lakh).

title: Can member withdraw entire amount through money order?
content: Q: Can member withdraw entire amount through money order? A: No. All payments are made electronically through NEFT or CBS (Core Banking Solutions).

title: Can pension be paid by money order or cheque?
content: Q: Can pension be paid by money order or cheque? A: No. Pension is payable through designated banks notified for each region through Core Banking Solution.

title: What is the Universal Account Number (UAN)?
content: Q: What is the Universal Account Number (UAN)? A: Universal Account Number (UAN) is a 12-digit number allotted to each PF subscriber by linking it to the member's active PF account number. From December 2016, any new member is allotted a UAN linked to the establishment's code number. UAN is permanent and does not change with change of employment.

title: Who allots the Universal Account Number?
content: Q: Who allots the Universal Account Number? A: The UAN is allotted by EPFO on the request of the Employer and populated in the Employer's login at the unified portal www.unifiedportal-emp.epfindia.gov.in. The UAN can also be generated by any individual using their Aadhaar if UAN is not already generated.

title: What are the advantages of having a UAN?
content: Q: What are the advantages of having a UAN? A: Once the member has activated UAN on the unified portal, they can: Download/Print Updated Passbook anytime, Download/Print UAN Card, List all Member IDs to UAN, File online transfer claim on OTCP, and Update KYC information.

title: How can I know the status of my UAN?
content: Q: How can I know the status of my UAN? A: The facility is available at https://unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in

title: How can I know the balance in my PF account?
content: Q: How can I know the balance in my PF account? A: Please activate your UAN and check/download your passbook at https://passbook.epfindia.gov.in/MemberPassBook/Login

title: How to check status of PF settlement/advance/transfer application?
content: Q: How to check status of PF settlement/advance/transfer application? A: Use the URL https://passbook.epfindia.gov.in/MemClaimStatusUAN/ for checking claim status.

title: Can PF member submit claims without employer attestation?
content: Q: Can PF member submit claims without employer attestation? A: Yes. Members can submit Form-19, 10C and 31 without employer attestation. The member must ensure UAN is activated and at least bank account and Aadhaar KYC are approved by the Employer using Digital Signature Certificate.

title: What is the Composite Claim Form?
content: Q: What is the Composite Claim Form? A: EPFO has consolidated settlement claim forms. One composite claim form (Aadhaar and Non-Aadhaar) replaces Forms 19, 10C, and 31, and UAN Forms 19, 10C, and 31. Another composite form replaces existing Form 20, 10D, and 5-IF.

title: Where can I get more information about EPFO?
content: Q: Where can I get more information about EPFO? A: Please visit EPFO Website: www.epfindia.gov.in

title: Who can be member of EPFO?
content: Q: Who can be member of EPFO? A: A person employed for wages in any kind of work (manual or otherwise) in or in connection with work of an establishment covered under the EPF & MP Act, 1952, who gets wages directly or indirectly from the employer, including persons employed through a contractor.

title: Who can be a member?
content: Q: Who can be a member? A: An employee of a covered establishment, if not excluded, is compulsorily a member of the EPF Scheme. An excluded employee is one whose pay at the time of being a member exceeds Rs.15,000 per month.

title: Can a member contribute at higher rate above 12% on voluntary basis?
content: Q: Can a member contribute at higher rate above 12% on voluntary basis? A: Yes. Members can contribute at a higher rate voluntarily.

title: What is the contribution rate for a member?
content: Q: What is the contribution rate for a member? A: An employee contributes 12% of Basic wages + Dearness allowance + Retaining allowance in EPF. The employer also pays 12% of pay, out of which 8.33% is diverted to Pension Fund and 3.67% to EPF.

title: Does a member contribute to EPS and EDLI?
content: Q: Does a member contribute to EPS and EDLI? A: No. The employer pays 12% out of which 8.33% is diverted to Pension Fund. An employer also pays 0.5% of Pay in EDLI Scheme. The employee does not contribute to EPS or EDLI.

title: What are the benefits available to EPF members?
content: Q: What are the benefits available to EPF members? A: Benefits include: (a) Advances for illness, marriage, education, housing, unemployment, natural calamity, physical handicap, pre-retirement; (b) Final settlement on retirement or 2 months after leaving service; (c) Pension after retirement subject to eligibility; (d) Insurance in case of death while in service.

title: What are the different types of non-refundable advances?
content: Q: What are the different types of non-refundable advances? A: Non-refundable advances are available under: Para 62 (Life Insurance), Para 68-B/BC/BD (Purchase of House/Flat), Para 68-BB (Repayment of housing loan), Para 68-H (Factory closure), Para 68HH (Unemployment), Para 68-J (Illness), Para 68-K (Marriage/Education), Para 68-L (Natural calamity), Para 68-M (Electricity cut), Para 68-N (Physically handicapped), Para 68-NN (Pre-retirement at 54+), Para 68-NNN (Varishta Pension Bima Yojana). Eligibility and amount limits vary per provision.

title: What documents are required for advances?
content: Q: What documents are required for advances? A: No document is required to be submitted for most non-refundable advances.

title: How to get grievances redressed?
content: Q: How to get grievances redressed? A: Lodge grievance online at https://epfigms.gov.in. Enter UAN/Establishment/PPO number, select grievance category and description, upload supporting documents. A unique registration number is generated and sent to mobile/email. The portal allows tracking status, adding documents, and providing feedback via star ratings.

title: What is KYC?
content: Q: What is KYC? A: KYC (Know Your Customer) is member's data updation to improve EPFO services. KYC details include PAN, Aadhaar, and Bank Account details. These should be updated on the EPFO Member Portal.

title: How can I update KYC?
content: Q: How can I update KYC? A: Member can update KYC details online in EPFO's UAN Portal at https://unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in

title: What are the benefits of KYC?
content: Q: What are the benefits of KYC? A: Claim can be submitted through online mode without attestation of the employer. Member can view monthly contribution statement by logging on UAN portal.

title: What are the KYC documents that can be submitted?
content: Q: What are the KYC documents that can be submitted? A: KYC documents include: National Population Register, Aadhaar, Permanent Account Number (PAN), Bank Account Number, Passport.

title: How to apply for correction in KYC/member details?
content: Q: How to apply for correction in KYC/member details? A: Member needs UAN. Login to EPFO UAN portal and update KYC by uploading necessary documents online. Online request for correction in name, date of birth, and gender has been introduced.

title: What is UAN?
content: Q: What is UAN? A: UAN (Universal Account Number) is a unique 12-digit number allotted to a member. It is permanent and remains valid throughout the life of a member. It does not change with change of employment. UAN helps in automatic transfer of Funds and PF withdrawals.

title: Who can generate UAN and how to get UAN?
content: Q: Who can generate UAN and how to get UAN? A: Your Employer can generate UAN. In case of change in employment, the previously allotted UAN may be provided to the employer.

title: EPF Act withdrawal provisions
content: A member can withdraw full PF balance after being unemployed for 2 months (60 days) from the date of leaving service. This is governed by Para 69(1) of the EPF Scheme, 1952 under Section 75(1) of EPF Act. Eligibility conditions: Member must be unemployed for at least 2 months. Minimum service requirements apply for different withdrawal types.

title: EPF Scheme settlement rules
content: Processing time: Claims are required to be settled within 20 working days as per EPF Scheme. UAN must be active and seeded with Aadhaar. KYC (Aadhaar and bank account) must be verified and approved by employer.

title: Form 19 Final PF Settlement Instructions
content: Form 19 is used for final settlement. Online process: Login to unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in, go to Online Services > Claim, verify bank account, click Proceed, select Form 19, enter amount and address, get Aadhaar OTP, submit. Offline process: Submit Composite Claim Form (Aadhaar/Non-Aadhaar) to Regional PF office.

title: Form 31 Partial withdrawal (advances) instructions
content: Form 31 is for non-refundable advances (partial withdrawals). No documents are required for most advances. ILLNESS (Para 68-J): Hospitalization > 1 month. Amount max 6 months basic wages+DA. MARRIAGE/EDUCATION (Para 68-K): 7 years membership, balance > Rs.1000. Amount 50% employee share. PRE-RETIREMENT (Para 68-NN): Age 54+, max 90% total balance. UNEMPLOYMENT (Para 68HH): Unemployed > 1 month, max 75% balance.

title: Advance withdrawal for COVID and medical emergencies
content: COVID-19 / EPIDEMIC (Para 68-L): Non-refundable advance available once during declared epidemic. Amount: 75% of PF balance or 3 months' wages/DA, whichever is less.

title: Housing loan advance Para 68B withdrawal
content: HOUSING Purchase/Construction (Para 68-B): Eligibility: 5 years membership, employee share > Rs.1,000. Amount: 24 months wages+DA, total PF balance, or total cost (lowest). HOUSING Loan Repayment (Para 68-BB): 10 years membership. Amount: 36 months wages+DA, total balance, or outstanding loan (lowest).

title: Official FAQ on PF withdrawal
content: PF not settled in 20 days: File complaint on EPFiGMS website or approach RPFC. Time limit to withdraw: 2 months waiting period applies for resignation, not superannuation. Without employer attestation: Use Composite Claim form Aadhaar if UAN is active and Aadhaar/Bank KYC is employer-approved.

title: EPFO KYC guidelines circular
content: KYC (Know Your Customer) requires linking Aadhaar, PAN, and Bank Account to UAN. Updated KYC enables online claim filing without employer attestation. Employer must digitally approve KYC. MINIMUM KYC includes mobile number linked to Aadhaar.

title: Aadhaar seeding instructions for members
content: Method 1: Login to member portal > Manage > KYC > Select Aadhaar > Enter Aadhaar number > Save > Employer approves. Method 2: e-KYC Portal on EPFO website or UMANG app for self-validation without employer if details match exactly. Name mismatch requires Joint Declaration.

title: PAN linking instructions
content: Login to member portal > Manage > KYC > Select PAN > Enter PAN number > Save > Employer approves. PAN is critical for withdrawal above Rs.50,000 for service under 5 years to avoid 20% max marginal TDS rate.

title: Bank account verification process
content: Login to member portal > Manage > KYC > Enter bank account number and IFSC code > OTP sent to Aadhaar-linked mobile > Employer approves. (SBI verification is direct). Cannot change bank account while a claim is pending.

title: Employer KYC approval process
content: Employer must approve KYC using Digital Signature Certificate (DSC) or Aadhaar-based e-Sign. Status becomes 'Digitally approved by the employer'. If employer is unavailable/closed, member can submit KYC request to field office attested by authorized officials.

title: FAQ on KYC issues
content: KYC update benefits: File claims without employer attestation. Invalid details: Use Joint Declaration for mismatch correction. Employer unresponsive: approach HR natively, escalate via EPFiGMS.

title: Form 13 PF transfer on job change
content: Form 13 is used to transfer PF when changing jobs. Online prerequisites: UAN activated, Aadhaar+Bank seeded, Date of Exit updated. Login > Online Services > One Member - One EPF Account (Transfer Request) > Select previous Member ID > Submit. No physical Form 13 needed if done online.

title: UAN-based auto transfer circular
content: UAN-linked members with Aadhaar verification benefit from auto-transfer. Transfer initiates automatically when the first month contribution is received in the new account from the new employer.

title: Transfer claim processing timeline
content: Transfer status updates from Pending with employer to Accepted by employer, then Pending at Field Office. Processing time targets 20 working days. Only one transfer request per previous Member ID allowed.

title: FAQ on PF transfer
content: Why transfer: Past service transfers over. Total service >= 5 years saves TDS on withdrawal. Service > 10 years enables pension. What transfers: Employee and Employer EPF shares. Pension amount does NOT transfer, only pensionable service history transfers.

title: Revised TDS rules on PF withdrawal 2024
content: TDS applicable if service < 5 years AND withdrawal exceeds Rs.50,000. Resident rate with valid PAN: 10%. Resident without valid PAN: 20%. Non-resident: 30% plus 4% cess. No TDS if service >= 5 years or transfer of funds.

title: TDS rules on PF withdrawal 2022 (Superseded)
content: Older TDS circular defining 30% penalty rate for missing PAN (changed to 20% in 2024 for residents). Withdrawn rules.

title: Form 15G instructions TDS exemption declaration
content: Submit Form 15G to avoid TDS. Eligibility: age below 60, total income below taxable limit (Rs.2.50 lakh). 2 copies required. Valid PAN mandatory to submit.

title: Form 15H instructions for senior citizens
content: Submit Form 15H to avoid TDS for Senior Citizens (age 60+) with income below Rs.3 lakh taxable limit. Valid PAN mandatory.

title: Income Tax Act Section 192A TDS on PF
content: Section 192A governs tax deduction at source from EPF balances. Legally mandates threshold of Rs.50,000 and 10% base rate, protecting members with 5+ years service.

title: Budget 2024 circular affecting PF taxation
content: Updates to maximum marginal rate for unlinked PANs from 30% to 20%. Updates to EPF tax exemptions.

title: FAQ on TDS and Form 15G
content: Check effective_date on TDS circulars. Rs.50k threshold applies per annum. TDS deducted will reflect in Form 26AS. Submit 15G promptly while claiming.

title: Employer Not Paying PF — Remedies and Actions
content:
When an employer deducts PF from employee wages but does not deposit with EPFO, there are several remedies available.
        
        HOW TO DETECT DEFAULT:
        - Check monthly contribution in e-passbook (activate UAN at unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in)
        - Receive SMS on registered mobile when monthly contribution is credited
        - Annual PF Statement of Account shows default periods
        
        REMEDIES FOR EMPLOYEE:
        1. File online grievance at https://epfigms.gov.in with salary slip showing PF deduction
        2. Approach Regional PF Commissioner with documentary evidence
        3. Appear before Commissioner in 'Nidhi Apke Nikat' program (10th of every month)
        
        LEGAL ACTIONS EPFO CAN TAKE AGAINST EMPLOYER:
        - Attachment of Bank Accounts (under Section 8B of EPF & MP Act)
        - Recovery from Debtors of employer
        - Attachment and Sale of employer's properties
        - Arrest and Detention of the Employer
        - Action under Section 406/409 IPC and Section 110 CrPC
        - Prosecution under section 14 of EPF & MP Act, 1952
        - Penal interest under section 7Q
        - Penal damages under section 14B
        
        IMPACT ON MEMBER:
        - PF amount will be paid only to the extent realised from employer
        - Member still receives full interest on amount actually deposited
        - Pension is GUARANTEED even if employer defaults on pension contribution
        - EDLI benefit is payable only for death while in service
        
        EMPLOYER CANNOT:
        - Deduct employer's share from employee wages (Criminal offence)
        - Reduce wages on account of PF payment (Prohibited under Section 12)
        - Recover outstanding dues from PF amount payable to member (Prohibited)
        - Attach PF amount against any liability (Protected under Section 10)
        
        PRINCIPAL EMPLOYER'S DUTY:
        Principal Employer is responsible for contractor's EPF compliance under Section 8A. Can recover EPF dues from amount payable to contractor.
conditions: IF employer_defaulted == true THEN file_grievance OR approach_RPFC

title: EPFiGMS Grievance Filing Process
content:
EPFiGMS (EPF i-Grievance Management System) is the official EPFO grievance portal at https://epfigms.gov.in.
        
        HOW TO FILE GRIEVANCE:
        Visit https://epfigms.gov.in > Enter UAN/Establishment/PPO number OR select 'Others' category > Fill grievance category and description > Upload supporting documents > Submit.
        A unique registration number is generated and sent via SMS and email.
        
        TRACKING STATUS:
        Use the registration number at epfigms.gov.in to check/track grievance status and disposal.
        
        INTERACTIVE FEATURES:
        - Complainant can add additional documents
        - EPFO offices can ask for documents and seek further inputs
        - Feedback on quality of redressal via star ratings
        
        WHEN TO USE EPFIGMS:
        - PF not settled within 20 working days
        - Employer not approving KYC despite requests
        - KYC rejected — reasons and resolution
        - Employer not paying PF contributions
        - Claim stuck or rejected
        - Transfer claim not processed
        
        ALTERNATIVE GRIEVANCE CHANNELS:
        - Regional PF Commissioner (in-person)
        - 'Nidhi Apke Nikat' program: 10th of every month at EPFO offices
        - Toll-free helpline: 14470 (24x7)
        - UMANG App
        - Social media: @socialepfo (Twitter/Facebook)
        - WhatsApp helpline number (check website for current number)
        
        REGIONAL OFFICE CONTACT:
        Visit https://www.epfindia.gov.in/site_en/Contact_us.php > Click Zonal Office > Click Regional Office for contact details.

title: International Workers — EPF Rules and SSA Guide
content:
International Workers (IW) are covered under Para 83 of the EPF Scheme, 1952.
        
        WHO IS AN INTERNATIONAL WORKER:
        1. Indian employee having worked or going to work in a foreign country with which India has a Social Security Agreement (SSA), eligible for SSA benefits
        2. Foreign national (non-Indian passport holder) working for an establishment in India covered under EPF & MP Act, 1952
        
        EXCEPTION (Excluded Employee):
        - Detached IW contributing to Social Security of home country and has Certificate of Coverage (COC) for specified period
        - IW contributing to social security of country of origin with bilateral agreement exempting social security contributions
        
        NEPALESE AND BHUTANESE NATIONALS are deemed Indian Workers (Proviso under Para 83). UAN can be generated only based on Aadhaar.
        
        NO SALARY CAP: No ceiling on salary for IW PF contribution (unlike Rs.15,000 for domestic workers).
        
        NO MINIMUM DAYS: Every eligible IW must be enrolled from the first date of employment in India.
        
        SSA COUNTRIES (India has signed 19 operational SSAs):
        Belgium, Germany, Switzerland, Denmark, Luxembourg, France, South Korea, Netherlands, Hungary, Finland, Sweden, Czech Republic, Norway, Austria, Canada, Australia, Japan, Quebec, Portugal. Brazil SSA signed but not yet operational.
        Detachment periods vary: 2-6 years depending on country.
        
        CERTIFICATE OF COVERAGE (COC):
        Certificate issued by EPFO for Indian workers going to SSA countries, certifying they are covered under Indian social security. Exempts them from contributing in the host country. Apply online at IW Portal on EPFO website. Regional PF Commissioner is the competent authority.
        
        PF WITHDRAWAL FOR IW:
        - SSA country nationals: On ceasing employment in India
        - Non-SSA country nationals: Only on retirement at age 58+ or permanent incapacity
        - SSA country nationals can receive payment in Indian bank account or home country bank
        - Non-SSA: Only Indian bank account or through employer
        
        PENSION FOR IW FROM SSA COUNTRIES:
        Withdrawal benefit under EPS only if not eligible for pension (total service < 10 years even with totalization).
        Non-SSA: No withdrawal benefit from EPS.
        Pensioners from SSA countries can receive pension in home country (exportability provision).
conditions: IF nationality != Indian AND ssa_country == true AND coc_available == true THEN excluded_employee
IF nationality != Indian AND ssa_country == false THEN mandatory_coverage
IF nationality == Indian AND working_abroad AND ssa_country == true THEN eligible_for_COC

title: Employees' Pension Scheme (EPS) 1995 — Complete Guide
content: Employees' Pension Scheme (EPS) 1995 provides for pension, family pension, and disability pension for EPF members. ELIGIBILITY: - Must be an EPF member - Employees drawing basic wages ≤ Rs.15,000 (new employees joining after 01.09.2014 earning > Rs.15,000 are NOT eligible for EPS) - No age restriction for EPF, but employee joining at 58 or above cannot become EPS member PENSION FORMULA: Monthly Pension = (Pensionable Salary × Pensionable Service) / 70 Pensionable Salary = Average of last 60 months' salary (for those retiring after 01.09.2014) Pensionable Salary = Average of last 12 months' salary (for those with date of commencement before 01.09.2014) TYPES OF PENSION: 1. Superannuation Pension: Attaining 58 years of age 2. Early/Reduced Pension: Leaving employment between age 50-57 (2% reduction per year before 58) 3. Disability Pension: Permanent total disability during employment, regardless of age/service 4. Family Pension (widow/widower): 50% of member pension, till death or remarriage 5. Children Pension: 25% of widow pension, for 2 children, till age 25 6. Orphan Pension: 75% of widow pension when widow/widower is not alive MINIMUM PENSION: Rs.1,000 per month (as per 2014 amendment) SCHEME CERTIFICATE: Issued when member has ≥10 years service and has not attained 58. Member with <10 years service can optionally take Scheme Certificate to carry forward service. WITHDRAWAL BENEFIT (instead of pension): Available only when: Service < 10 years AND member has not availed Scheme Certificate. Based on table in EPS 1995 (wages × table factor for number of years). PENSION PAYMENT: - Through designated nationalised banks across India - Digital Life Certificate (DLC) via Aadhaar annually (at CSCs, bank branches, Post Offices, IPPB, PF offices) - Physical Life Certificate: Annually to bank where pension is drawn PENSION ARREARS (Commutation Restoration): Those who commuted pension before 25.09.2008 under Para 12A get restored to normal pension after 15 years from date of commutation (per GSR No. 132(E) dated 20.02.2020, Para 12B). PRAYAAS SCHEME: Members superannuating within 3 months are guided to submit pension claims 1 month before retirement for PPO issuance on date of retirement. HIGHER PENSION (Supreme Court Order 04.11.2022): Members who contributed on
conditions: IF service_years >= 10 AND age >= 58 THEN eligible_superannuation_pension
IF service_years >= 10 AND age >= 50 AND age < 58 THEN eligible_early_pension
IF disabled == true AND in_service == true THEN eligible_disability_pension
IF member_deceased == true AND contribution_received >= 1 month THEN family_eligible_for_pension
IF service_years < 10 AND scheme_cert_not_held THEN eligible_withdrawal_benefit

title: Employees' Pension Scheme (EPS) 1995 — Complete Guide
content: No. 132(E) dated 20.02.2020, Para 12B). PRAYAAS SCHEME: Members superannuating within 3 months are guided to submit pension claims 1 month before retirement for PPO issuance on date of retirement. HIGHER PENSION (Supreme Court Order 04.11.2022): Members who contributed on actual wages above Rs.15,000 under Para 26(6) are eligible for higher pension. Submit Application for Validation of Option / Joint Options. Joint Request and undertaking from employer required.

title: EPF Contribution Rates — All Schemes
content:
EPF Contribution rates under the EPF & MP Act, 1952.
        
        STANDARD RATES:
        - Employee contribution: 12% of Basic wages + Dearness Allowance + Retaining Allowance + Cash value of food concession
        - Employer contribution: 12% of same pay components
        
        EMPLOYER'S 12% SPLIT:
        - 8.33% → Employees' Pension Scheme (EPS) Fund (capped at Rs.15,000 wage = Rs.1,250/month)
        - 3.67% → Employees' Provident Fund (EPF) Account
        
        ADDITIONAL EMPLOYER CONTRIBUTIONS:
        - EDLI: 0.5% of wages (capped at Rs.15,000 wage = Rs.75/month)
        - EPF Administrative charges: 0.5% of wages (subject to minimum of Rs.75 per month for non-defaulting establishments; Rs.25 for exempted establishments)
        
        WAGE CEILING:
        - Standard: Rs.15,000 per month (Basic + DA + Retaining allowance)
        - For International Workers: No ceiling — contribution on full salary
        - Voluntary higher contribution possible under Para 26(6) with permission
        
        VOLUNTARY CONTRIBUTION:
        - Member can contribute more than 12% voluntarily (VPF - Voluntary Provident Fund)
        - Total voluntary + mandatory can be up to Rs.15,000 per month at standard ceiling
        - For Para 26(6) members contributing on actual higher wages
        
        INTEREST RATE:
        - Declared annually by Central Government
        - Recent rates: 8.65% (2016-17), 8.55% (2017-18), 8.65% (2018-19), 8.5% (2019-20 to 2021-22), 8.15% (2022-23), 8.25% (2023-24)
        - Credited on monthly running balance basis (compound interest)
        - Accounts earn interest up to age 58. Accounts inoperative after 36 months of no contribution post-retirement
        
        COVID REDUCED RATE (Historical):
        - 10% contribution rate for wage months May 2020, June 2020, July 2020 (SO 1513(E) dated 18.05.2020)
        - Not applicable now — standard 12% rate applies
        
        PAYMENT DUE DATE:
        - 15th of the following month via ECR (Electronic Challan cum Return)
        - Online payment via internet banking (SBI/nationalised banks) or PayGov
        - Due date does not change even if 15th is a public holiday

title: Inoperative EPF Accounts — Rules and Actions
content:
An EPF account is classified as Inoperative when no contribution has been received for 3 years after:
        - Retirement
        - Permanent migration abroad
        - Death
        
        INTEREST ON INOPERATIVE ACCOUNTS:
        - At present, all accounts earn interest up to age 58 of the member
        - After account becomes inoperative, no further interest is credited
        - Account becomes inoperative after 36 months from date of retirement (55 years+) or permanent migration
        
        WHAT TO DO WITH INOPERATIVE ACCOUNT:
        - If still employed in covered establishment: Get amount transferred to new account (online or offline Form 13)
        - If retired: Withdraw the amount by submitting withdrawal claim
        
        UNCLAIMED PF (SENIOR CITIZENS FUND):
        Unclaimed EPF amounts are transferred to Senior Citizens Welfare Fund (SCWF) after 7 years of becoming inoperative. However, members can still claim this amount from EPFO.
        
        HOW TO CLAIM INOPERATIVE ACCOUNT:
        - Trace the account using establishment name or PF number
        - Contact the employer for details
        - Submit withdrawal claim to concerned Regional PF Office with proper KYC documentation
        
        PASSBOOK FOR INOPERATIVE ACCOUNTS:
        - For exempted establishments: Passbook not visible on UAN portal; contact establishment directly
        - For non-exempted: Login to UAN portal passbook section
conditions: IF contribution_stopped == true AND months_since_last_contribution >= 36 THEN account_inoperative
IF account_inoperative == true AND still_employed == true THEN transfer_recommended
IF account_inoperative == true AND retired == true THEN withdraw_recommended

title: Higher Pension on Actual Wages — Supreme Court Order 2022
content:
The Supreme Court of India on 04.11.2022 directed that members who had contributed on actual wages above the wage ceiling under Para 26(6) of the EPF Scheme are eligible for higher pension.
        
        ELIGIBILITY:
        - Members who contributed to EPS on wages exceeding the statutory wage ceiling (Rs.5,000/6,500/15,000 as applicable)
        - Must have submitted joint request under Para 26(6) OR employer contribution on higher wages must be verifiable from records
        
        PROOF OF JOINT OPTION UNDER PARA 26(6):
        1. Permission under Para 26(6) uploaded at time of application
        2. OR verification that: (a) Employer share contributed on higher wages, (b) Admin charges paid, (c) PF account updated with interest, AND any of these documents: wage details by employer, salary slip authenticated by employer, joint request copy, EPFO letter indicating PF contribution on higher wages
        
        APPLICATION PROCESS:
        Submit 'Application for Validation of Option / Joint Options' online. Field offices obtain required documents from employer. Applications cannot be rejected only for lack of Para 26(6) proof.
        
        PENSION CALCULATION FOR HIGHER PENSION:
        - Date of commencement of pension BEFORE 01.09.2014: Pensionable salary = average of last 12 months
        - Date of commencement of pension ON/AFTER 01.09.2014: Pensionable salary = average of last 60 months
        - Formula remains: Pension = (Pensionable Salary × Pensionable Service) / 70
        
        MEMBERS YET TO RETIRE:
        Pension will be calculated based on EPS provisions existing on the date of commencement of pension.
        
        CONTRIBUTION DEMAND:
        Members opting for higher pension must pay the difference in EPS contribution (8.33% of actual wages minus 8.33% of wage ceiling) with interest. This can be adjusted from PF accumulations.
        
        PENSION ARREARS:
        Arrears of pension will be paid in accordance with existing process complying with TDS provisions.
conditions: IF contributed_on_higher_wages == true AND joint_option_proof_available == true THEN eligible_higher_pension
forms: Application for Validation of Option / Joint Options

title: ECR and Employer Compliance — Complete Guide
content:
Electronic Challan cum Return (ECR) is the electronic system for employer compliance under EPF & MP Act, 1952.
        
        WHAT ECR CONTAINS:
        - Number of employees employed
        - UAN of each employee
        - Gross/EPF/EPS/EDLI wages
        - Contributions under all three schemes
        - Administrative charges due
        - Wage disbursal date
        - Number of excluded employees and their gross wages
        
        ECR SUBMISSION PROCESS:
        Login to https://unifiedportal-emp.epfindia.gov.in/epfo/ > ECR section > Upload/enter ECR data > Generate challan > Pay online (SBI/nationalised banks internet banking or PayGov or scheduled banks).
        
        REVAMPED ECR (Re-Engineered ECR):
        Applicable from wage month September 2025. Redesigned version with enhanced features.
        
        PAYMENT DUE DATE:
        15th of the following month. No change even if 15th is a public holiday.
        
        ECR DOES NOT LAPSE:
        An ECR created but not paid remains available for future payment reference.
        
        DSC/E-SIGN FOR EMPLOYER:
        Required for KYC approval, transfer claim attestation, etc.
        One-time registration approval from Regional EPFO office required.
        Employer can register Aadhaar-based e-Sign if DSC dongle unavailable.
        
        FORM 5A (Ownership Return):
        Must contain particulars of all branches and departments including code numbers. Must be updated online using approved DSC. Required for PMGKY benefit claims.
        
        UAN GENERATION BY EMPLOYER:
        Individual: Login > Member > Register-Individual > Enter employee details (Aadhaar, Name, DOB, Gender, Father/Husband Name, DOJ, EPF wages, Bank, PAN) > Save > Approve.
        Bulk: Login > Member > Register-Bulk > Upload file (format available as Help File) > Approve.
        
        EMPLOYER RESPONSIBILITIES:
        - Enroll all eligible employees from day one
        - Ensure KYC seeding and approval
        - Pay contributions by 15th of each month
        - Update date of joining and date of exit
        - File correct ECR with accurate wage data
        - Maintain Form 5A with complete establishment details

title: PMGKY — Pradhan Mantri Garib Kalyan Yojana EPF Package
content:
PMGKY (Pradhan Mantri Garib Kalyan Yojana) EPF package was announced as part of COVID-19 relief for wage months March 2020, April 2020, and May 2020.
        
        ELIGIBILITY (ESTABLISHMENT):
        - Registered under EPF & MP Act, 1952
        - Total employees up to 100
        - 90% or more of employees drawing wages less than Rs.15,000 per month
        
        ELIGIBILITY (EMPLOYEE):
        - Working in eligible establishment
        - Earning monthly wages less than Rs.15,000
        - UAN seeded with Aadhaar
        - EPF and EPS member whose contributions received for any period during September 2019 to February 2020 in any eligible establishment
        
        BENEFIT:
        - Central Government pays 24% of monthly wages (12% employee + 12% employer) directly into UAN
        - Employee gets full take-home salary (no EPF deduction)
        - Employer saves 12% of wages
        
        HOW TO CLAIM:
        - Employer disburses full salary without deducting EPF from eligible employee wages
        - Employer files ECR for the month (includes eligible and ineligible employees)
        - Challan shows Central Government relief portion separately
        - Employer pays only ineligible employee contributions and EDLI/admin charges
        - Central Government directly credits contributions to eligible UANs
        
        FORM 5A:
        Must be updated with all branches/departments details for PMGKY eligibility validation.
        
        NOTE: PMGKY benefit was for 3 specific months in 2020. Not currently active.
        Historical benefit — included for claim status queries and past contribution records.

title: EPF Nomination — Complete Guide (e-Nomination and Form 2)
content:
Nomination ensures quick settlement of EPF/EPS/EDLI benefits to family in case of member's death.
        
        FORMS FOR NOMINATION:
        Form 2: Prescribed for EPF, EPS, and EDLI nomination. Covers family and nomination details.
        
        ONLINE NOMINATION (E-NOMINATION):
        - Login to member portal > eNomination section
        - Aadhaar-based e-Sign required (NOT employer approval)
        - Photo upload of nominee required
        - Nominee's Aadhaar required
        - Can submit e-Nomination from anywhere, anytime
        
        OFFLINE NOMINATION:
        - Fill Form 2 manually
        - Submit through employer
        
        WHO CAN BE NOMINATED:
        - For EPF: Family includes spouse, children (married or unmarried), dependent parents, deceased son's widow and children
        - For Pension: Family includes spouse, children
        - Unmarried member: Can nominate any person, but nomination becomes void on acquiring a family
        
        IMPORTANT RULES:
        - Nomination can only be changed during member's lifetime; cannot be changed after death
        - After death, no new nominee can be added if valid nomination exists
        - Employer approval NOT required for e-Nomination
        - Keep nomination updated (especially after marriage, birth of child)
        
        IF NO NOMINATION:
        - EPF: Paid to family members in equal shares under Para 70(ii); if no family, to legally entitled persons
        - EPS: Paid to family (spouse first, then children); if no family, to nominee; if no nominee, to dependent parents
        
        DATE OF BIRTH OF NOMINEE:
        Based on Aadhaar in e-Nomination. Change requires fresh e-Nomination after Aadhaar correction.
        
        BENEFITS OF NOMINATION:
        In case of member's death, family gets EPF/Pension/EDLI without delays.

title: Member Data Correction — Joint Declaration Process
content:
Correction in member profile (Name, DOB, Gender, Aadhaar, Father/Husband Name, Marital Status, Nationality, Date of Joining, Date of Leaving, Reason of Leaving) is done via Joint Declaration.
        
        ONLINE PROCESS:
        Login to member portal > Joint Declaration (JD) Tab > Fill correction request > Upload prescribed documents (as per SOP on JD) > Submit > Employer digitally approves.
        
        DEATH OF MEMBER:
        JD form signed by Nominee as mentioned in Form 2. Then processed per extant SOP on JD.
        
        DATE OF BIRTH CORRECTION DOCUMENTS (any one acceptable):
        - Birth certificate from Registrar of Births and Deaths
        - School/education certificate
        - Service records of Central/State Government organisation
        - Passport
        - Any other reliable Government-issued document
        - Medical certificate from Civil Surgeon + affidavit (if no other proof)
        - Aadhaar/e-Aadhaar: Accepted for change up to ±3 years from recorded DOB
        
        NAME CHANGE AFTER MARRIAGE:
        Joint request online by member + employer approval. Documents: Marriage certificate + proof that only name changed (school records with father's name/DOB, pre-marriage PAN, etc.).
        
        DATE OF EXIT CORRECTION:
        If wrong, employer must request concerned PF Office for correction.
        Member can self-update Date of Exit after 60 days of leaving (via Manage > Mark Exit on member portal).
        
        WHEN EMPLOYER IS UNAVAILABLE (Closed establishment):
        Submit request to concerned field office with attest by authorised officials (as per para 10.18 of MAP Vol. II).
        
        AADHAAR CORRECTION:
        Must contact UIDAI. For corrections visit https://uidai.gov.in or nearest Aadhaar Service Centre.

title: KYC Rejection — Common Reasons, Resolution Steps, and Re-Submission Guide
content: KYC (Know Your Customer) documents can be rejected by EPFO or employer for several reasons. Understanding the exact rejection reason is the first step to resolution. HOW TO CHECK KYC STATUS AND REJECTION REASON: Login to https://unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in/memberinterface/ > Manage > KYC. Status will show: Pending / Digitally Approved by Employer / Rejected / Verified by UIDAI. Click on the rejected entry to see the reason. COMMON KYC REJECTION REASONS AND RESOLUTION: 1. NAME MISMATCH (Aadhaar vs PF Records): Reason: Name on Aadhaar does not match name registered in EPFO records. Resolution: Submit Joint Declaration (JD) form online to correct name in PF records first, then re-link Aadhaar. OR update Aadhaar to match PF records via UIDAI. Documents needed: marriage certificate if name changed, school certificate or passport showing original name. 2. DATE OF BIRTH MISMATCH (Aadhaar vs PF Records): Reason: Date of birth on Aadhaar differs from DOB in EPFO system. Resolution: Submit Joint Declaration for DOB correction with supporting document (birth certificate, school certificate, passport, or Aadhaar itself if within ±3 years of PF record). Employer must approve JD. 3. AADHAAR NOT LINKED TO MOBILE NUMBER: Reason: OTP cannot be sent during KYC verification as Aadhaar has no mobile registered. Resolution: Visit nearest Aadhaar Seva Kendra (ASK) to register/update mobile number in Aadhaar. Or visit UIDAI website at https://uidai.gov.in. After updating, retry KYC after 48-72 hours. 4. BANK ACCOUNT IFSC INCORRECT: Reason: Bank IFSC code entered is invalid, or bank branch has merged and IFSC changed. Resolution: Get correct current IFSC from your bank's branch. Re-enter bank account details. For SBI accounts — verification is automatic; for others, employer approval required. 5. EMPLOYER NOT APPROVING KYC (Most Common): Reason: Employer HR/admin has not logged into EPFO employer portal to digitally approve KYC. Resolution Step 1: Contact employer HR/admin directly, ask them to login to https://unifiedportal-emp.epfindia.gov.in > Member > KYC Approval > Approve with DSC or e-Sign. Resolution Step 2 (if employer unresponsive after 7 days): File grievance at https://epfigms.gov.in selecting category "KYC". Resolution Step 3 (closed establishment): Submit KYC documents physically to concerned PF field office with attestation by
conditions: IF kyc_status == rejected AND reason == name_mismatch THEN submit_joint_declaration
IF kyc_status == rejected AND reason == employer_not_approved AND days_waiting > 7 THEN raise_epfigms_grievance
IF kyc_status == rejected AND reason == aadhaar_mobile_missing THEN visit_uidai
IF kyc_aadhaar == verified_by_uidai AND kyc_bank == employer_approved THEN eligible_online_claim

content: Approval > Approve with DSC or e-Sign. Resolution Step 2 (if employer unresponsive after 7 days): File grievance at https://epfigms.gov.in selecting category "KYC". Resolution Step 3 (closed establishment): Submit KYC documents physically to concerned PF field office with attestation by authorised official. 6. AADHAAR ALREADY LINKED TO ANOTHER UAN (Duplicate UAN): Reason: Aadhaar was previously linked to a different UAN. Resolution: Merge duplicate UANs using 'One Member - One EPF Account' facility in Member Interface. All previous Member IDs should be linked under a single UAN. Then re-link Aadhaar. 7. PAN ALREADY IN USE (Duplicate PAN): Reason: PAN is linked to another UAN. Resolution: Verify your PAN details. Contact EPFO field office if PAN was incorrectly linked to another account. HOW TO RE-SUBMIT KYC AFTER REJECTION: Step 1: Delete the rejected KYC document (click Delete on rejected entry in KYC section). Step 2: Re-enter the corrected details and re-upload / re-link. Step 3: Wait for employer approval (for non-Aadhaar documents) or UIDAI verification (for Aadhaar). Step 4: If employer is unresponsive for more than 7 days, raise grievance on EPFiGMS selecting "KYC not approved by employer" as category. AADHAAR SEEDING WITHOUT EMPLOYER (Self KYC via OTP): If your name, DOB, and gender on Aadhaar exactly match PF records, you can link Aadhaar using OTP without employer involvement: Login to member portal > Manage > KYC > Select Aadhaar > Enter Aadhaar number > Get OTP > Submit. If details match UIDAI records, status becomes 'Verified by UIDAI' directly — no employer step needed. MINIMUM KYC REQUIREMENT FOR ONLINE CLAIMS: Bank Account (employer approved) + Aadhaar (UIDAI verified) + Active UAN. PAN KYC is additionally needed to avoid higher TDS on withdrawals.

title: Partial PF Withdrawal (Form 31) — Online Step-by-Step Process
content: Form 31 is used for non-refundable advance / partial withdrawal from EPF for specific approved purposes. This is different from full withdrawal (Form 19). WHEN TO USE FORM 31 (not Form 19): - You are still employed OR recently unemployed (less than 2 months) - You need money for illness, marriage, education, housing, or pre-retirement (not full settlement) - You want to withdraw up to 75% while unemployed for more than 1 month but less than 2 months ELIGIBILITY QUICK REFERENCE BY PURPOSE: Purpose | Min Service | Max Amount | Allowed Times Illness | None | 6 months wages or employee's share (lesser) | No limit Marriage | 7 years | 50% of employee's share | Max 3 times Education | 7 years | 50% of employee's share | Max 3 times Housing purchase| 5 years | 24 months wages or PF balance (lesser) | Once Loan repayment | 10 years | 36 months wages or PF balance (lesser) | Once Pre-retirement | 54+ age | 90% of total PF balance | Once Unemployment | Any | 75% of total PF balance | Once (for 1-2 months unemployed) HOW TO FILE FORM 31 ONLINE (Step-by-Step): Step 1: Login to https://unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in/memberinterface/ → Enter UAN, password, and captcha. Step 2: Verify KYC is complete. → Go to Manage > KYC. Confirm bank account shows 'Digitally Approved by Employer' and Aadhaar shows 'Verified by UIDAI'. If not, complete KYC first. Step 3: Navigate to Online Services > Claim (Form-31, 19, 10C & 10D). Step 4: Verify bank account. → Your KYC-approved bank account appears. Enter last 4 digits to verify. Click 'Verify'. Step 5: Click 'Yes' on the certificate of undertaking. Step 6: Click 'Proceed for Online Claim'. Step 7: Select 'PF ADVANCE (FORM 31)' from the dropdown. Step 8: Select the purpose from the list (Illness / Marriage / Education / Housing etc.). Step 9: Enter required amount and your current address. Step 10: Upload required document if asked. → Most purposes require NO document upload. The portal will show if a document is required. Step 11: Click 'Get Aadhaar OTP'.
conditions: IF purpose == marriage AND service_years >= 7 AND employee_balance > 1000 THEN eligible_form31_marriage
IF purpose == housing AND service_years >= 5 THEN eligible_form31_housing
IF purpose == pre_retirement AND age >= 54 THEN eligible_90_percent_advance
IF unemployment_months >= 1 AND unemployment_months < 2 THEN eligible_75_percent_advance_form31
IF kyc_bank_approved == true AND kyc_aadhaar_verified == true THEN online_claim_possible
