title: TDS on PF Withdrawal — Practical Guide 2024 (When, How to Avoid, Form 15G)
content: Practical guide on TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) for PF withdrawals, reflecting rules effective from April 2022 and updated in 2024. THE SIMPLE RULE — TWO CONDITIONS MUST BOTH BE TRUE FOR TDS: Condition 1: Total service is LESS than 5 years (counting service across all employers if PF was transferred) AND Condition 2: Withdrawal amount is MORE than Rs.50,000 If EITHER condition is false → NO TDS is deducted. COUNTING THE 5 YEARS: - Service includes all employers where PF was contributed AND transferred to your current account - Example: 3 years at Company A (transferred) + 2.5 years at Company B = 5.5 years total → NO TDS - If you did NOT transfer PF from previous employer, each account is treated separately - TDS applies per withdrawal claim, not per employer TDS RATES (for residential Indians with PAN): Scenario | TDS Rate PAN valid + service < 5 yrs + > 50k | 10% No valid PAN + service < 5 yrs + >50k | 20% Service 5+ years (regardless of amount)| 0% Amount ≤ Rs.50,000 | 0% HOW TO SUBMIT FORM 15G (to get zero TDS): WHO: Resident individual below 60 years whose estimated total income for the year is below Rs.2,50,000 (i.e., below basic exemption limit). WHEN: Submit BEFORE filing the withdrawal claim. Cannot be submitted after TDS is deducted. WHERE (Online): Login to member portal > Online Services > Claim > You'll see a checkbox/link to upload Form 15G before submitting the claim. Upload 2 copies. WHERE (Offline): Submit 2 physical copies along with claim form to PF office. REQUIREMENT: Must have valid PAN linked to UAN. Without PAN, Form 15G cannot be accepted and TDS will be at 20%. FORM 15H (for senior citizens 60+ years): Same process as 15G but for members aged 60 and above. Income threshold for Form 15H: Rs.3,00,000 (basic exemption for senior citizens). 2024 UPDATES — KEY CHANGES: 1. TDS on Transfer NOT applicable: Transfer of PF from one account to another is not treated as withdrawal — no TDS even if service < 5 years. 2. Advances/Partial
conditions: IF service_years < 5 AND withdrawal_amount > 50000 THEN TDS_applicable
IF form_15G_submitted == true AND pan_valid == true AND income_below_250000 THEN no_TDS
IF pan_missing == true AND TDS_applicable THEN tds_rate_20_percent
IF pan_valid == true AND TDS_applicable THEN tds_rate_10_percent

title: TDS on PF Withdrawal — Practical Guide 2024 (When, How to Avoid, Form 15G)
content: (basic exemption for senior citizens). 2024 UPDATES — KEY CHANGES: 1. TDS on Transfer NOT applicable: Transfer of PF from one account to another is not treated as withdrawal — no TDS even if service < 5 years. 2. Advances/Partial withdrawals (Form 31): TDS is NOT applicable on partial withdrawal advances. 3. Split accounts (EPF + VPF): TDS calculated on total taxable withdrawal, not split separately. 4. Non-resident (NRI) members: TDS at 30% (or DTAA rate if lower) — Form 15G/15H not available to NRIs. File Form 10F for DTAA benefit. WHAT HAPPENS AFTER TDS DEDUCTION: - Form 16A (TDS certificate) is issued by EPFO within 15 days from due date of TDS return - TDS can be claimed as credit in your Income Tax Return (ITR) - If your total income is below taxable limit, you can claim TDS refund from Income Tax Department EXAMPLE SCENARIOS: Scenario A: Service 4 years, balance Rs.80,000, valid PAN → TDS = 10% of Rs.80,000 = Rs.8,000. Submit Form 15G to avoid if income < Rs.2.5 lakh. Scenario B: Service 4 years, balance Rs.40,000, valid PAN → NO TDS (below Rs.50,000 threshold). Scenario C: Service 6 years, balance Rs.2,00,000 → NO TDS (service >= 5 years). Scenario D: Service 3 years, no PAN → TDS = 20% on amount above threshold. TAX YEAR NOTE: For withdrawal spanning two financial years, TDS is calculated in the year of withdrawal. Always provide latest Form 15G for the current financial year (April to March).
conditions: IF service_years < 5 AND withdrawal_amount > 50000 THEN TDS_applicable
IF form_15G_submitted == true AND pan_valid == true AND income_below_250000 THEN no_TDS
IF pan_missing == true AND TDS_applicable THEN tds_rate_20_percent
IF pan_valid == true AND TDS_applicable THEN tds_rate_10_percent

title: How to File a Grievance on EPFiGMS — Step-by-Step with Documents
content: Complete practical guide to filing a grievance on EPFiGMS (EPF i-Grievance Management System) at https://epfigms.gov.in. WHEN TO FILE A GRIEVANCE: - PF withdrawal claim not settled within 20 working days - KYC not approved by employer despite multiple requests (wait at least 7 days first) - Employer not depositing PF contributions - Transfer claim stuck or not processed - Claim rejected for reasons you disagree with or cannot resolve - Incorrect PF balance shown - UAN issues not resolved by normal means - Passbook not updated for more than 2 months after contribution STEP-BY-STEP GRIEVANCE FILING: Step 1: Go to https://epfigms.gov.in Step 2: Click 'Register Grievance'. Step 3: Select your type: → 'EPF Members/Pensioners' (for current/former employees) → 'Employer' (for establishments) → 'Others' (if not enrolled / UAN not available) Step 4: Enter your UAN (preferred) OR PF Member ID OR PPO Number (for pensioners). Step 5: Enter security PIN shown on screen and click 'Get Details'. Step 6: Verify your details (name, DOB, mobile) and confirm. Step 7: Select Grievance Type from dropdown (categories below). Step 8: Write grievance description in the text box — be specific: → Mention: date of claim submission, claim type (Form 19/31/13), claim ID if available, what you have already tried. → Example: "I submitted Form 19 online on 01-Jan-2024. Claim ID: XXXXX. It is now 30 working days and still shows 'Under Process'. I have not received any communication." Step 9: Upload supporting documents (JPG, PDF, up to 1MB each): → For stuck claims: Screenshot of claim status from member portal → For KYC not approved: Screenshot of KYC status showing pending → For employer non-deposit: Copy of salary slip showing PF deduction → For rejected claim: Screenshot of rejection reason → For wrong balance: Screenshot of passbook Step 10: Enter personal details (name, email, mobile). Step 11: Click 'Submit'. Note the Registration Number shown (also sent via SMS/email). GRIEVANCE CATEGORIES AND WHEN TO USE THEM: Category | Use When Claim Settlement | PF/pension/advance claim not settled in time KYC Related | Employer not approving KYC, KYC rejected Transfer/PF Account Related | Transfer

title: How to Claim EPS Pension — Form 10C (Withdrawal Benefit / Scheme Certificate) and Form 10D
content: Practical guide to claiming EPS (Employees' Pension Scheme) benefits — covering Form 10C for withdrawal benefit or scheme certificate, and Form 10D for monthly pension. WHICH FORM TO USE: Form 10C: Use when service is LESS than 10 years OR when you want Scheme Certificate to carry forward pension service. Form 10D: Use when service is 10 or more years AND you have attained 58 years (superannuation) or 50 years (early pension). Also used by family for family pension after member's death. ─── FORM 10C — WITHDRAWAL BENEFIT OR SCHEME CERTIFICATE ─── WHO IS ELIGIBLE FOR WITHDRAWAL BENEFIT (Form 10C): - Service between 6 months and 9 years 6 months (not yet 10 years) - Member must be unemployed for 2+ months (or superannuated) - Benefit: Lump sum amount based on a table (wages × factor from EPS 1995 table) WHO SHOULD TAKE SCHEME CERTIFICATE INSTEAD: - Any member with less than 10 years service who wants to preserve pension credit - If you plan to rejoin service: take Scheme Certificate — it carries forward your pension service - On next employment, surrender Scheme Certificate to new employer — service gets added HOW TO FILE FORM 10C ONLINE: Step 1: Login to https://unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in > Online Services > Claim (Form-31, 19, 10C & 10D). Step 2: Verify bank account (last 4 digits). Step 3: Click 'Proceed for Online Claim'. Step 4: Select 'PF AND EPS WITHDRAWAL (FORM 19 & 10C)' OR 'ONLY PF WITHDRAWAL (FORM 19)' depending on your intent. Step 5: If eligible, 'EPS SCHEME CERTIFICATE / WITHDRAWAL (FORM 10C)' appears separately — select it. Step 6: Choose either 'Withdrawal Benefit' or 'Scheme Certificate' — choose wisely as this is final. Step 7: Get Aadhaar OTP and submit. Important: If you are also withdrawing PF (Form 19), both can be submitted in the same session. SCHEME CERTIFICATE PRACTICAL NOTE: If you received Scheme Certificate in paper, and it is lost — contact the PF office where it was issued with evidence of your service. A duplicate can be issued. If you want to convert Scheme Certificate to Withdrawal Benefit —

IF service_years < 10 AND want_to_preserve_service THEN take_scheme_certificate
IF service_years >= 10 AND age >= 58 THEN eligible_form_10d_pension
IF service_years >= 10 AND age >= 50 AND age < 58 THEN eligible_early_reduced_pension
IF member_deceased AND contribution_received >= 1_month THEN family_eligible_form_10d

title: How to Transfer PF When Changing Jobs — Step-by-Step (Form 13 Online)
content: Practical step-by-step guide to transfer your PF when you change jobs. Transfer is always recommended over withdrawal to preserve service history, compounding interest, and pension benefit. WHY TRANSFER INSTEAD OF WITHDRAW: 1. Service continuity: Total service ≥ 5 years → no TDS on future withdrawal 2. Service continuity: Total service ≥ 10 years → eligible for EPS pension 3. Interest compounding: PF earns 8.25% (2023-24), doubles roughly every 8-9 years 4. No tax: Transfer is not a withdrawal — zero tax on transfer 5. Pension history: EPS service record carries over even though pension amount itself is not transferred WHEN TO INITIATE TRANSFER: Best time: After joining new employer and first contribution appears in new PF account (typically after 45 days of joining). You need: New PF Member ID OR new UAN-linked account to be active. PREREQUISITES (CHECKLIST): □ Old employer has marked your Date of Exit in EPFO system → If not: You can self-update after 60 days of leaving (Manage > Mark Exit in member portal) □ New employer has generated your UAN/Member ID and made at least 1 contribution □ Your UAN is active and has Aadhaar + bank account linked (KYC complete) □ Old employer had approved KYC (Aadhaar / bank) — needed for online transfer eligibility STEP-BY-STEP ONLINE TRANSFER (Form 13): Step 1: Login to https://unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in > Online Services > One Member - One EPF Account (Transfer Request). Step 2: Verify personal details and click 'Request for Transfer of Account'. Step 3: On the transfer page: → Previous Member ID: Select from the list OR enter manually → Current Member ID: Auto-populated if only one active account, or select the new one → Select who will attest: 'Previous Employer' or 'Present Employer' — choose whichever is easier. Present Employer is usually easier. Step 4: Get OTP (Aadhaar-linked mobile) and submit. Step 5: EPFO sends the request to the selected employer's portal for approval. → Employer must login and digitally approve using DSC/e-Sign within the specified time. Step 6: Track status via Online Services > Track Claim Status. → Status sequence: 'Pending with Employer' → 'Under

Step 6: Track status via Online Services > Track Claim Status. → Status sequence: 'Pending with Employer' → 'Under Process at Field Office' → 'Settled'. WHAT IF DATE OF EXIT IS NOT UPDATED BY OLD EMPLOYER: After 60 days from last working day: Login > Manage > Mark Exit > Select old PF Account Number > Enter Date of Exit + Reason (Resignation/Superannuation/etc.) > Get OTP (Aadhaar mobile) > Submit. This self-update is available only 60+ days after leaving. Before that, only employer can update it. WHAT TRANSFERS AND WHAT DOES NOT: Transfers: Employee share + Employer share + Interest (full PF corpus) Does NOT transfer: EPS pension amount — only the service history/record is carried over for pension calculation TRANSFER TIMELINES: Online claims: Usually 20 working days. Offline (exempted establishment): Can take 30-45 working days. If stuck beyond 30 working days: File grievance on EPFiGMS selecting 'Transfer/PF Account Related'. AUTO-TRANSFER FOR UAN MEMBERS: If your UAN is seeded with Aadhaar and bank, and your new employer's first contribution is received, auto-transfer may trigger without you needing to file Form 13 manually. Check member portal passbook to see if auto-transfer has happened. PHYSICAL/OFFLINE TRANSFER (when online not possible): Download Form 13 from https://www.epfindia.gov.in/site_en/Downloads.php. Fill with previous and current member details, get employer attestation. Submit to PF office where previous account is maintained. Exempted-to-Exempted establishment transfer is always offline — submit to respective Trust.
conditions: IF date_of_exit_updated == true AND new_employer_contribution_received == true AND kyc_complete == true THEN eligible_online_transfer
IF date_of_exit_not_updated AND days_since_leaving >= 60 THEN self_update_exit_date
IF old_establishment_type == exempted AND new_establishment_type == exempted THEN offline_transfer_only

title: EPF Passbook — How to Access and Interest Crediting
content:
EPF Passbook shows monthly contributions, transfer credits, and interest credited.
        
        HOW TO ACCESS PASSBOOK:
        - Login at https://unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in > View > Passbook > Enter UAN, password, captcha
        - Direct access: https://passbook.epfindia.gov.in/MemberPassBook/Login
        - Download: Go to Download menu > Download Passbook > PDF download link available
        - Via UMANG App > EPFO > Employee Centric Services > Passbook
        - Via missed call to 011-22901406
        - Via SMS to 7738299899 in 10 languages
        
        PASSBOOK NOT AVAILABLE:
        If working in an Exempted establishment, passbook is not available on UAN portal. Contact establishment directly for PF statement.
        
        INTEREST CREDITING:
        - Interest is credited on monthly running balance basis
        - Credited annually after Central Government declares rate
        - Delay in passbook update does not cause financial loss — interest is added to closing balance and becomes opening balance for next year
        - If member withdraws before interest is updated, due interest is auto-calculated and paid at claim settlement
        
        SERVICE HISTORY:
        Login to e-Passbook > Click on Service History to see employment details across multiple establishments.
        
        CLAIM STATUS:
        Track claim status at https://passbook.epfindia.gov.in/MemClaimStatusUAN/

title: EPF Act 1952 — Section 14B (penalty for non-deposit)
content: Section 14B of the EPF Act 1952 empowers the Central Provident Fund Commissioner or such other officer authorised to recover damages from an employer who defaults in the payment of any contribution to the Fund. The penalty can be up to 100% of the arrears.

title: How to report employer non-compliance — EPFO guide
content: If your employer deducts PF but fails to deposit into your account, you can report it via the EPFiGMS portal. Select grievance type 'Employer not depositing PF'. Provide evidence such as salary slips showing PF deduction.

title: EPFIGMS grievance portal guide
content: The EPFiGMS portal (epfigms.gov.in) allows members to file grievances online. Requires UAN or EPF number. Allows tracking grievance status. Recommended for PF withdrawal delays or employer non-compliance.

title: Circular on action against defaulting employers
content: Defines strict actions against defaulting employers, including attachment of bank accounts, FIRs under sections 406/409 IPC, and action under section 14 of the EPF act.

title: EPS 1995 — full scheme document
content: The Employees Pension Scheme, 1995 provides superannuation pension, retiring pension, permanent total disablement pension, widow or widower pension, children pension, and orphan pension.

title: EPS eligibility conditions
content: To be eligible for member pension under EPS 1995, an individual must have completed a minimum of 10 years of eligible service and attained the age of 58 years for superannuation pension, or 50 years for early (reduced) pension.

title: Form 10C instructions
content: Form 10C is used to claim EPS withdrawal benefits (if service is less than 10 years) or to apply for a Scheme Certificate (to carry forward pensionable service to a new job). Members can submit Form 10C online if UAN is verified.

title: Form 10D instructions
content: Form 10D is the application form for claiming monthly pension under EPS 1995. Can be submitted by the member upon reaching age 58 (or 50 for reduced pension), or by family members in the event of the member's death.

title: EPS pension calculation formula
content: Monthly Pension = (Pensionable Salary × Pensionable Service) ÷ 70. Pensionable Salary is the average of the last 60 months' wages. Maximum pensionable service considered is 35 years.

title: Supreme Court circular on higher pension option
content: Following the Supreme Court judgment, EPFO allowed eligible members to opt for a higher pension by contributing 8.33% of actual wages (above the statutory ceiling) to the Pension Fund. Eligible members had to file joint options before the deadline.

title: FAQ on EPS pension
content: Early pension is available from age 50 but reduced by 4% for every year short of 58. Minimum 10 years service required. Nominees receive pension if no eligible family member is alive.

title: UAN activation guide for members
content: Go to Member e-Sewa portal and click 'Activate UAN'. Enter UAN, Aadhaar, Name, DOB, and Mobile exactly as per Aadhaar. Get authorization pin on mobile and submit. UAN is necessary for all online PF services.

title: How to merge multiple PF accounts under one UAN
content: Members should have only one UAN. If holding multiple UANs, request 'One Member - One EPF Account' via the online transfer module to merge previous PF accounts into the active UAN. Inform your current employer to continue with the active UAN.

title: Name / date of birth correction process
content: Submit Joint Declaration form to correct Name, Gender, DOB, or DOJ. Aadhaar is mandatory evidence for corrections. For DOB corrections of more than 3 years, an additional document like birth certificate, education certificate, or passport is required.

title: Circular on UAN for international workers
content: International Workers must be issued a UAN and their passport details must be seeded. Certificate of Coverage (COC) applications are tightly linked with UAN and passport status.

title: EPFIGMS grievance categories and process
content: Grievance categories include Final Settlement, Transfer, Scheme Certificate, KYC issues, and Employer default. Members can attach up to three documents (PDF/JPG, max 1MB) during registration. Action taken report is uploaded by CPFC.

title: Grievance response timeline circular
content: EPFO targets a maximum 15 days redressal time for most grievances filed under EPFiGMS. Grievances should not be closed without substantial resolution and communicating the decision to the member.

title: How to check grievance status
content: To track a grievance, visit epfigms.gov.in and click 'View Status'. Enter the unique grievance registration number received via SMS/Email.

title: Union Budget 2024 — PF related announcements circular
content: Budget 2024 provisions confirm continuation of existing EPF interest rate of 8.25%. Updated marginal TDS rates apply for unlinked PAN.

title: Union Budget 2023 — PF related announcements circular
content: Previous budget circular reducing the highest TDS rate for non-PAN withdrawal from 30% to 20% in specific resident cases. Now superseded by 2024 circular.

title: Circular on PF contribution rate changes
content: Standard rate of contribution is 12% of basic wages, DA, and retaining allowance by both employee and employer. Under specific relaxations (e.g., COVID PMGKY), this rate was temporarily lowered to 10%, but has reverted to 12% for all standard establishments.

title: Circular on PF wage ceiling changes
content: The statutory wage ceiling for EPF contributions is currently Rs.15,000 per month. Employers are not required to contribute the employer share on amounts exceeding Rs.15,000 unless a Joint Option under Para 26(6) is adopted. This limit was increased from Rs.6,500 effective 01-09-2014.

title: EPFO Forms Reference — Complete List with Purpose and Submission
content:
Complete reference for EPFO forms and their uses.

WITHDRAWAL / SETTLEMENT FORMS:
Form 19: PF Final Settlement (Full withdrawal). Online if KYC complete.
Form 10C: Scheme Certificate (retain pension membership) OR Withdrawal Benefit (if service < 10 years). Online if KYC complete.
Form 10D: Monthly Pension claim. For members/family seeking EPS pension.
Form 31: Non-refundable Advance / Partial withdrawal. Online if KYC complete.
Form 20: Death case — PF withdrawal by nominee/legal heir.
Form 5-IF: EDLI (Insurance) benefit claim by nominee/legal heir.
Composite Claim Form (Aadhaar): Replaces Forms 19, 10C, 31 — can be submitted without employer attestation. Online and offline.
Composite Claim Form (Non-Aadhaar): Replaces Forms 19, 10C, 31 — requires employer attestation. Offline only.
Another Composite Form: Replaces Forms 20, 10D, 5-IF for death/pension cases.

TRANSFER FORMS:
Form 13 / Form 13(R): PF transfer request from previous to current account. Online preferred; physical only for exempted-to-exempted.
Annexure-K: Transfer from exempted trust — details sent by trust to EPFO/receiving trust.

MEMBER REGISTRATION / KYC:
Form 11 (New): Declaration of previous EPF service on new joining. Enables auto-transfer.
Form 2: Nomination form for EPF, EPS, and EDLI. Family and nominee details.
Form 3A/ECR Copy: Contribution card. Member can demand from employer.

TAX FORMS:
Form 15G: Declaration for zero TDS if total income below Rs.2.5 lakh (non-senior citizens, any age). Needs PAN.
Form 15H: Declaration for zero TDS if total income below Rs.3 lakh (Senior Citizens age 60+). Needs PAN.
Form 10F: Non-resident member — for DTAA rate benefit.
Form 10FB: Certificate of residence for DTAA claim.

EMPLOYER FORMS:
Form 5A: Ownership return — must contain all branches/departments. DSC required.
ECR: Electronic Challan cum Return — monthly contribution return by employer.

INTERNATIONAL WORKERS:
COC Application (online): Certificate of Coverage for Indian workers going to SSA countries. Online at IW Portal.
IW Forms: IW1, IW2 (through employer portal) for International Worker details.

PENSION SCHEME:
Form 10B: Pension claim for family pension (widow/widower) in case of death.
Form 10C-A: Scheme Certificate request.

KEY SUBMISSION NOTES:
- Forms 19, 10C, 31 can be submitted online WITHOUT employer attestation when UAN has Aadhaar + Bank Account approved by employer
- For online claims, OTP is sent to Aadhaar-linked mobile number
- Physical forms require cancelled cheque with printed member name + bank account + IFSC
- No revenue stamp required on claim forms
- No money order or cheque for PF settlement — all payments via NEFT/CBS

title: EPF Scheme Amendment 2014 — Wage Ceiling Revised to Rs.15,000
content: GSR 422(E) dated 22.08.2014 — The Central Government amended Para 2(f) of the EPF Scheme 1952 and Para 2(f) of EPS 1995. The wage ceiling for mandatory EPF/EPS coverage was revised from Rs.6,500 to Rs.15,000 per month, effective 01.09.2014. KEY CHANGES: 1. New threshold: Employees with basic wages + DA up to Rs.15,000 are mandatorily covered. 2. Employees joining after 01.09.2014 earning above Rs.15,000 are excluded from EPS-1995 membership. Their employer's 8.33% contribution goes entirely to EPF (not EPS). 3. Members who were already EPS members before 01.09.2014 continue as EPS members regardless of wages. 4. Para 26(6) option retained: Employees earning above Rs.15,000 may voluntarily join EPF by giving joint option with employer within 6 months of joining. 5. Members who joined under Para 26(6) above wage ceiling after 01.09.2014 are NOT eligible for EPS membership. IMPACT: A new employee joining with salary Rs.18,000 after September 2014 — their full 12% employer contribution goes to EPF (no EPS). A similar employee who joined before September 2014 and was already EPS member continues under EPS with contribution capped at Rs.15,000 wage.
conditions: IF join_date >= 2014-09-01 AND basic_wages > 15000 THEN not_eligible_for_EPS
IF join_date < 2014-09-01 AND EPS_member == true THEN continue_as_EPS_member

title: EPS Amendment 2014 — Minimum Pension Rs.1,000 and 60-Month Pensionable Salary
content: GSR 609(E) dated 22.08.2014, effective 01.09.2014 — Amendment to Employees' Pension Scheme 1995. KEY CHANGES: 1. MINIMUM PENSION: Rs.1,000 per month minimum pension guaranteed for all existing and future EPS pensioners. 2. PENSIONABLE SALARY CALCULATION CHANGE: - Before amendment: Average of last 12 months' salary - After amendment: Average of last 60 months' salary (for members whose pension commences on or after 01.09.2014) - Members who retired before 01.09.2014: Old 12-month average applies - This change affects pension amount — 60-month average is typically lower than 12-month for members with recent salary increases 3. HIGHER PENSION OPTION: - Para 26(6) joint option must be submitted within 6 months of joining employment - Both employer and employee must sign - EPFO must accept the option - Employer must have contributed on actual higher wages throughout service IMPACT ON PENSION CALCULATION EXAMPLE: Member with last 5 years salary: 12k, 13k, 14k, 15k, 15k (monthly in thousands) 12-month average (old rule): Rs.15,000 60-month average (new rule): Rs.(12+13+14+15+15)/5 = Rs.13,800 Pension difference = (15000 × service)/70 vs (13800 × service)/70

title: EDLI Scheme Amendment 2021 — Maximum Assurance Benefit Rs.7 Lakh
content: GSR 220(E) dated 28.04.2021 — Amendment to Employees' Deposit Linked Insurance Scheme 1976. KEY CHANGES EFFECTIVE 28.04.2021: 1. MAXIMUM ASSURANCE BENEFIT: Increased from Rs.6,00,000 to Rs.7,00,000. 2. MINIMUM ASSURED BENEFIT: Rs.2,50,000 (irrespective of PF balance) — introduced to protect low-balance members. 3. CALCULATION FORMULA: Assurance benefit = 35 times the monthly wages (subject to wage ceiling Rs.15,000) + PF balance up to Rs.1,50,000. - Maximum from formula: 35 × 15,000 + 1,50,000 = Rs.6,75,000 (capped at Rs.7,00,000) - Minimum guaranteed: Rs.2,50,000 PREVIOUS FORMULA (before 2021 amendment): - 30 times monthly wages + PF balance, maximum Rs.6,00,000 ELIGIBILITY (unchanged): - Death of EPF member while IN SERVICE - Benefit payable to nominee/legal heir - Not applicable for death after leaving service EXAMPLE: Member dies with PF balance Rs.80,000, last wage Rs.12,000. Formula: (35 × 12,000) + min(80,000, 1,50,000) = 4,20,000 + 80,000 = Rs.5,00,000. Minimum guaranteed: Rs.2,50,000. Since Rs.5,00,000 > Rs.2,50,000, benefit = Rs.5,00,000.

title: EPF COVID-19 Non-Refundable Advance 2020 — Para 68-L Amendment
content: SO 1513(E) dated 18.05.2020 — Para 68L of EPF Scheme 1952 amended for COVID-19 relief. NON-REFUNDABLE ADVANCE PROVISION: - Available to ALL EPF members affected by COVID-19 pandemic - Amount: Lower of (a) 75% of total PF balance, or (b) 3 months' Basic Wages + DA - Can be withdrawn ONCE only (not available again) - No minimum service requirement - No documentation required to prove COVID impact - Available only when Government has declared epidemic/pandemic REDUCED CONTRIBUTION RATE (separate provision): - SO 1513(E) + SO 2120(E): EPF contribution rate reduced to 10% (from 12%) - For: All non-government establishments and non-public sector undertakings - For wage months: May 2020, June 2020, July 2020 - Both employee and employer contribution reduced to 10% - Government's contribution for PMGKY scheme continued at 12% STATUS AS OF 2024: This provision has lapsed. Standard 12% rate applies. Para 68-L remains in the Scheme for future government-declared epidemics but the COVID-specific variant is no longer active. Members who availed COVID advance cannot avail again for the same account. HISTORICAL IMPORTANCE: If your passbook shows a claim from May-July 2020 for 75% advance, that was the COVID advance. If you withdrew it, your current balance is lower and you cannot claim that advance again.
conditions: IF pandemic_declared == true AND advance_not_taken_before THEN eligible_covid_advance

title: Section 192A Amendment 2022 — TDS on EPF Withdrawal Updated Rules
content: GSR 604(E) dated 31.08.2021, effective 01.04.2022 — Amendment to Section 192A of Income Tax Act read with Finance Act 2020 changes. CURRENT TDS RULES (effective April 2022): WHEN TDS APPLIES: Service < 5 years AND withdrawal amount > Rs.50,000. WHEN TDS DOES NOT APPLY: - Service >= 5 years (combined across employers if PF transferred) - Withdrawal amount <= Rs.50,000 - Transfer of PF (not withdrawal) - Partial advance (Form 31) - Form 15G / 15H submitted with valid PAN - Service terminated due to ill health or employer winding up (beyond employee control) TDS RATES: Resident with valid PAN: 10% (Section 194A rate) Resident without PAN or invalid PAN: 20% (Section 206AA — higher of 20% or applicable rate) Non-resident with valid PAN: 30% or DTAA rate (whichever beneficial); + 4% Health & Education Cess Non-resident without PAN: 30% + cess; no threshold exemption FORM 15G / 15H: Form 15G: For residents below 60 years. Total estimated income < Rs.2,50,000 for the FY. Must have PAN. Form 15H: For senior citizens 60+. Total estimated income < Rs.3,00,000 for the FY. Must have PAN. Must be submitted BEFORE the withdrawal claim. Requires 2 copies. Cannot retroactively avoid TDS. EMPLOYER'S OBLIGATION: Employer (EPFO) must deduct TDS, deposit by 7th of following month (30th April for March). Issue Form 16A (TDS certificate) within 15 days of due date of quarterly TDS return. Report in Form 26Q (residents) or Form 27Q (non-residents). MEMBER'S RECOURSE IF TDS DEDUCTED: Claim TDS refund through Income Tax Return (ITR) filing. TDS credited as advance tax paid — reduces final tax liability or generates refund. Check Form 26AS on IT portal to verify TDS credit.
conditions: IF service_years < 5 AND withdrawal_amount > 50000 THEN TDS_applicable
IF form_15G_valid == true AND pan_valid == true THEN no_TDS
IF pan_missing == true AND TDS_applicable == true THEN tds_rate_20_percent
IF pan_valid == true AND TDS_applicable == true THEN tds_rate_10_percent

title: Higher Pension on Actual Wages — SC Order Nov 2022 and EPFO Implementation
content: Supreme Court Order dated 04.11.2022 in SLP (C) No. 8658-8659 of 2019 (EPFO vs Sunil Kumar B. and others). EPFO Circular No. Pension/2022/54877/15149 dated 29.12.2022 for implementation. SUPREME COURT DIRECTION: Members who contributed to EPS on actual wages exceeding the wage ceiling, having exercised joint option under Para 26(6) of EPF Scheme 1952, are entitled to pension on those actual wages. EPFO was directed to allow such members to file for validation of their joint option and receive higher pension. WHO IS ELIGIBLE: Condition 1: You and your employer contributed to EPS on wages exceeding the statutory ceiling (Rs.5,000 / Rs.6,500 / Rs.15,000 as applicable in your period). Condition 2: A joint request under Para 26(6) was submitted (or contributions on higher wages are verifiable from records even without a formal joint request document). Condition 3: You have not already availed EPS withdrawal benefit. HOW TO APPLY: Online only. Login to EPFO member portal > Online Services > Higher Pension Application. OR via employer's portal. Field offices to obtain employer's certificate endorsing service records. PENSIONABLE SALARY: - If pension commenced before 01.09.2014: Average of last 12 months' actual wages - If pension commenced on/after 01.09.2014: Average of last 60 months' actual wages Formula unchanged: Pension = (Pensionable Salary × Pensionable Service) / 70 CONTRIBUTION RECOVERY: Difference between 8.33% of actual wages and 8.33% of wage ceiling, for the entire service period, plus interest — will be recovered from PF accumulations. This can significantly reduce your PF corpus. IMPORTANT: Calculate the impact before applying — higher pension may not always be beneficial if it drains PF corpus needed immediately. DEADLINE: Application deadline was extended multiple times. Check EPFO website for current deadline. EPFO helpline 14470 for status of higher pension applications.
conditions: IF contributed_on_higher_wages == true AND joint_option_proof == true THEN eligible_higher_pension

title: EPF Interest Rate 2023-24 — 8.25% per annum
content: Central Board of Trustees (CBT) in its 233rd meeting recommended EPF interest rate of 8.25% per annum for 2023-24. Government of India accepted recommendation. Gazette notification issued June 2024. INTEREST RATE HISTORY: 2023-24: 8.25% (current) 2022-23: 8.15% 2021-22: 8.10% (lowest in 4 decades) 2020-21: 8.50% 2019-20: 8.50% 2018-19: 8.65% 2017-18: 8.55% 2016-17: 8.65% HOW INTEREST IS CALCULATED: - On monthly running balance (closing balance of previous month) - Credited annually after Government declares rate - Compounded — interest earns interest in subsequent years - No delay in interest accrual: Even if passbook update is delayed, interest is calculated and credited from correct date TAX ON EPF INTEREST (post Finance Act 2021): - Employee contributions up to Rs.2.5 lakh per year: Interest tax-free - Employee contributions above Rs.2.5 lakh per year (for VPF/voluntary contributions): Interest on excess is taxable as income from other sources - Employer contribution: Always tax-free at withdrawal (if service >= 5 years) INOPERATIVE ACCOUNT INTEREST: - Account becomes inoperative 36 months after retirement (age 55+) or permanent migration abroad - No interest credited after account becomes inoperative - Interest up to the date of inoperativeness is credited in full

title: R.C. Gupta v. Regional PF Commissioner (SC 2016) — EPS Higher Pension Para 26(6)
content: Case: R.C. Gupta & Ors. v. Regional Provident Fund Commissioner & Ors. (Supreme Court 2016). Citation: (2016) 13 SCC 539. Court: Supreme Court of India. Relevance: CRITICAL — directly governs higher pension eligibility. FACTS: Members and their employer had been contributing to EPS on actual wages above the statutory ceiling under Para 26(6) of EPF Scheme. EPFO later refused to grant pension on actual wages, insisting pension be capped at ceiling wage. HELD: The Supreme Court ruled in favour of members. Members who had exercised option under Para 26(6) and whose employer had contributed on actual wages are entitled to pension on those actual wages. EPFO cannot unilaterally cap pension to ceiling wages when contributions were made on higher wages. KEY PRINCIPLE: The exercise of option under Para 26(6) and actual payment of contribution on higher wages together constitute a valid joint option. A formal written joint option in addition is not always required if contributions were demonstrably made on higher wages. IMPACT: This judgment was the direct basis for the Supreme Court's 2022 direction to EPFO to allow higher pension applications. If you contributed on higher wages before the wage ceiling was applicable or above it, this case supports your higher pension claim. LIMITATION: The 2022 Supreme Court order (EPFO v. Sunil Kumar B.) modified R.C. Gupta to clarify the proof requirements and application timeline. Members must now apply via EPFO's online portal within the deadline.

title: EPFO v. Sunil Kumar B. (SC Nov 2022) — Higher Pension Final Direction
content: Case: Employees' Provident Fund Organisation & Ors. v. Sunil Kumar B. & Ors. SLP (C) No. 8658-8659 of 2019. Supreme Court of India. Order dated 04.11.2022. Relevance: THE most important recent EPF judgment — governs higher pension for millions. BACKGROUND: Multiple High Courts had directed EPFO to allow higher pension. EPFO challenged. Consolidated at Supreme Court. HELD: 1. Members who had contributed on actual wages above wage ceiling under Para 26(6) may apply to validate their joint option and receive higher pension. 2. Members who could not exercise option earlier due to EPFO's refusal are given fresh opportunity. 3. Pensionable salary: Average of 60 months for pension commencement after 01.09.2014; 12 months for earlier. 4. EPFO to recover difference in EPS contribution (8.33% on actual wages minus 8.33% on ceiling) plus interest from PF accumulations. 5. Former employees already receiving pension on ceiling wages may also apply for revision. 6. Deadline for application: Originally 4 months from order, extended multiple times by EPFO (check current EPFO website). PROOF OF CONTRIBUTION: Court accepted that even without formal joint option document, contributions on higher wages verified through: (a) employer's wage records/salary slips, (b) actual PF contribution records showing higher wages, (c) employer's certificate. KEY PRACTICAL POINT: Applying for higher pension reduces PF corpus (because past EPS shortfall is recovered). Members should calculate net benefit before applying — especially those needing lump sum from PF soon.
conditions: IF contributed_on_higher_wages == true AND application_deadline_not_passed THEN apply_for_higher_pension

title: Regional PF Commissioner v. Shiv Kumar Joshi (SC 2000) — What is 'Pay' for EPS
content: Case: Regional Provident Fund Commissioner v. Shiv Kumar Joshi (2000) 2 SCC 43. Court: Supreme Court of India. Relevance: Defines which salary components count toward pensionable wages. HELD: 'Pay' for EPS pensionable salary means Basic Pay + Dearness Allowance + Retaining Allowance. HRA (House Rent Allowance), CCA (City Compensatory Allowance), overtime allowance, and production incentives are NOT included. PRINCIPLE: Only universally paid components that go toward basic cost of living are counted. Variable and conditional components are excluded from EPF/EPS wage base. WHY THIS MATTERS: If your employer has been computing EPF on a lower wage base (e.g., excluding DA), your PF contributions and pension calculation may be understated. Employer must include DA in EPF wage base if DA is paid as part of regular wages. If DA is not paid as a separate component (e.g., in many private sector companies that pay a consolidated CTC), EPF is typically on basic salary only. PRACTICAL IMPLICATION: If you are disputing your employer's EPF contribution amount, this case establishes that Basic + DA is the mandatory base. File grievance on EPFiGMS or approach RPFC if employer is under-computing.
conditions: IF employer_computing_EPF_on_basic_only AND da_is_paid THEN employer_may_be_undercontributing

title: Bridge and Roof Co. v. Union of India (SC 1963) — EPF Act Constitutionality
content: Case: Bridge and Roof Co. (India) Ltd. v. Union of India (1963) 3 SCR 978. Court: Supreme Court of India. Constitution Bench. Relevance: Foundation case — establishes the legal basis of EPF Act. HELD: The Employees' Provident Funds and Miscellaneous Provisions Act 1952 is constitutionally valid. The employer's obligation to contribute to EPF is a statutory obligation, not a deduction from employee wages. It is a social security measure and falls within Parliament's legislative competence. PRINCIPLE: PF is a form of deferred wage — it belongs to the employee even though physically held by EPFO. Employer's share of PF is an additional obligation over and above wages. KEY PRACTICAL IMPLICATION: 1. Employer CANNOT treat employer's PF contribution as a deduction from CTC/wages — this is illegal. 2. Employee's take-home wages cannot be reduced on account of PF payment (confirmed by Section 12 of EPF Act). 3. PF accumulations are protected from attachment by courts under Section 10 of EPF Act. IF EMPLOYER DEDUCTS EMPLOYER'S SHARE FROM SALARY: This is a criminal offence under Section 14 of EPF & MP Act. File complaint with RPFC and police (Section 406/409 IPC).

title: Bhavna Bhatt v. RPFC (Delhi HC 2018) — PF Withdrawal on Death Without Nomination
content: Case: Bhavna Bhatt v. Regional Provident Fund Commissioner (Delhi High Court, 2018). Relevance: Governs death claim process when no valid nomination exists. FACTS: Member died without having filed Form 2 nomination. Widow approached EPFO for PF settlement. EPFO demanded succession certificate from civil court, causing delay. HELD: Legal heir certificate from competent authority (Tehsildar / Revenue Officer / SDM) or court affidavit is sufficient for PF claim settlement when no nomination exists. EPFO cannot insist on succession certificate from civil court in all cases — this causes undue hardship to families. PROCESS FOR DEATH CLAIM WITHOUT NOMINATION: 1. Widow/widower can claim using legal heir certificate from SDM/Tehsildar 2. Children can claim with their birth certificates + death certificate of member 3. EPFO processes under Para 70(ii) of EPF Scheme — equal shares among family members 4. EPS family pension: Automatically payable to spouse. Submit Form 10D. 5. EDLI benefit: Form 5-IF. Same documents. DOCUMENTS NEEDED FOR DEATH CLAIM: - Death certificate of member - Legal heir certificate (SDM/Tehsildar) OR succession certificate from court if no legal heir cert available - Bank account details of claimant - Aadhaar of claimant (for UAN-linked accounts) - Age proof of widow/children - Form 20 (EPF), Form 10D (EPS pension), Form 5-IF (EDLI)
conditions: IF member_deceased AND nomination_exists THEN pay_to_nominee
IF member_deceased AND nomination_not_exists THEN legal_heir_certificate_required

title: Principal Employer EPF Liability for Contractor Employees — SC Principle
content: Consolidated principle from multiple Supreme Court judgments including EPFO v. Fancy Weaving Works (2020) and related contractor liability cases. LEGAL POSITION: Under Section 8A of EPF & MP Act 1952, the Principal Employer is responsible for ensuring EPF compliance for ALL employees working on the premises, including those employed through contractors. KEY RULES: 1. If contractor fails to enroll employees or deposit PF, PRINCIPAL EMPLOYER IS LIABLE. 2. Principal employer cannot escape liability by claiming the contractor was independent. 3. Principal employer CAN recover the EPF amount paid from the contractor. 4. Workers cannot be denied EPF simply because contractor is unregistered. WHAT PRINCIPAL EMPLOYER MUST DO: - Ensure all eligible contract employees are enrolled under EPF from day 1 - Verify contractor deposits monthly contributions (check via Establishment Search on EPFO website) - If contractor defaults, deposit EPF on behalf of contract workers and recover from contractor FOR CONTRACT WORKERS: If your PF is not deposited by contractor: File EPFiGMS grievance AGAINST THE PRINCIPAL EMPLOYER, not just the contractor. RPFC has powers to recover from principal employer. Bring: salary slip, ID card, any communication showing employment at the establishment. RELEVANT PROVISION: Section 8A EPF & MP Act — 'Notwithstanding anything in any contract, the principal employer shall be liable to pay the amount payable under this Act in respect of an employee employed by or through a contractor.'
conditions: IF contract_employee == true AND pf_not_deposited THEN file_grievance_against_principal_employer

title: VPF Interest Taxable Above Rs.2.5 Lakh Contribution — Finance Act 2021
content: Finance Act 2021 introduced taxation on EPF interest for high-contribution members, effective April 2022. WHAT CHANGED: Before April 2022: All EPF/VPF interest was completely tax-free, regardless of contribution amount. After April 2022: Interest on EMPLOYEE contribution exceeding Rs.2,50,000 per year is taxable as 'Income from Other Sources'. For government employees: The threshold is Rs.5,00,000 per year (because employer does not contribute; higher limit given). WHAT THIS MEANS PRACTICALLY: - Standard employee at Rs.15,000 wage contributes 12% = Rs.1,800/month = Rs.21,600/year — well below Rs.2.5 lakh. NOT AFFECTED. - High-salary employees contributing large VPF amounts: Interest on the excess above Rs.2.5 lakh annual contribution is taxable. - Example: Employee contributes Rs.4,00,000/year to EPF+VPF. Interest on Rs.(4,00,000 - 2,50,000) = Rs.1,50,000 is taxable. EMPLOYER CONTRIBUTION: Not included in the Rs.2.5 lakh threshold — employer's contribution of 12% remains tax-free. HOW IT'S TRACKED: EPFO maintains two sub-accounts within EPF: Account A: Taxable portion (contributions above Rs.2.5 lakh/year from April 2022 onwards) Account B: Non-taxable portion Interest accrued in Account A is reported in Form 16 / Form 26AS as taxable interest. Source: Budget 2021 Finance Bill, Section 10(11) and 10(12) amendment, confirmed in CBDT Circular.

title: EPFO Services Available on UMANG App — Complete List 2024
content: UMANG (Unified Mobile Application for New-age Governance) app provides access to all major EPFO services on mobile without needing to login to the full web portal. HOW TO ACCESS: Download UMANG from Google Play Store / Apple App Store. Search 'EPFO' after login. SERVICES AVAILABLE ON UMANG: 1. View EPF Passbook (balance check) 2. Raise claim — Form 19 (final settlement), Form 10C (pension/scheme cert), Form 31 (advance) 3. Track claim status 4. KYC update (Aadhaar, PAN, bank account) 5. UAN activation 6. e-Nomination filing 7. Mark date of exit (after 60 days of leaving) 8. Transfer claim (Form 13) initiation 9. Raise EPFiGMS grievance 10. View pension passbook (for pensioners) 11. Generate UAN (if not already generated) 12. Download UAN card 13. Link Aadhaar via OTP (self KYC without employer) UMANG LOGIN: Use your UAN and EPFO member portal password. Same credentials. ADVANTAGE OVER WEB PORTAL: Works on low-end smartphones. Faster for simple tasks like balance check. OTP-based Aadhaar linking works offline-equivalent. UMANG TOLL-FREE: 1800-11-5246. NOTE: For complex claims (higher pension, death claims, closed establishment issues), the full web portal at unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in is still recommended.

title: Auto-Transfer of PF on Job Change via UAN-Aadhaar Seeding
content: EPFO introduced auto-transfer feature for UAN-seeded members changing jobs. HOW IT WORKS: When an employee with Aadhaar-seeded UAN joins a new employer and the first EPF contribution is received in the new Member ID, the system checks if the same UAN has other active Member IDs with balance. If yes, a transfer request is automatically generated. CONDITIONS FOR AUTO-TRANSFER: 1. UAN must be Aadhaar-seeded (UIDAI verified) 2. Date of exit from previous employer must be updated (either by employer or self-marked after 60 days) 3. New employer must have made at least 1 contribution in the new Member ID 4. Both previous and new establishments must be unexempted (EPFO-maintained, not private trust) 5. Previous account must have a balance HOW TO CHECK IF AUTO-TRANSFER HAPPENED: Login to member portal > View > Transfer Passbook / Passbook. If a large credit appears shortly after joining new employer, it's the auto-transfer. OR: Online Services > Track Claim Status > look for a settled transfer claim. IF AUTO-TRANSFER DID NOT TRIGGER: File manual Form 13 transfer request via Online Services > One Member One EPF Account. Common reasons auto-transfer fails: Date of exit not updated, previous establishment is exempted, Aadhaar not seeded, UIDAI name mismatch. MULTIPLE OLD ACCOUNTS: If you have 3+ old accounts, only the most recent eligible one may auto-transfer. File manual transfer requests for older accounts separately.
conditions: IF aadhaar_seeded == true AND date_of_exit_updated AND new_contribution_received THEN auto_transfer_may_trigger
IF auto_transfer_failed AND establishment_type == unexempted THEN file_manual_form13

title: Nidhi Apke Nikat — EPFO Open House Programme for Member Grievances
content: 'Nidhi Apke Nikat' (Your Funds Near You) is a monthly open house programme run by EPFO for direct grievance redressal. WHEN: 10th of every month (or next working day if 10th is a holiday) WHERE: All EPFO Regional / Sub-Regional offices across India TIME: Typically 10 AM to 5 PM WHO CAN ATTEND: Any EPF member, pensioner, or their legal representative WHAT ISSUES CAN BE RAISED: - PF claim not settled (bring reference number) - Pension not started or incorrect amount - KYC rejection — employer not responding - Old accounts not appearing - Grievance not resolved on EPFiGMS - Any EPFO-related issue requiring in-person resolution WHAT TO BRING: - UAN / Member ID / PPO number - Aadhaar card (original + photocopy) - Bank passbook (original + photocopy) - All relevant correspondence with EPFO - Employer contact details (if employer is the issue) - Any reference numbers from previous complaints OFFICER PRESENT: Regional PF Commissioner or designated senior officer attends in person and gives on-the-spot decisions where possible. This is the fastest in-person escalation channel available for complex cases that are not resolving via EPFiGMS or helpline. No appointment needed.

title: Digital Life Certificate for Pensioners — Jeevan Pramaan Process 2024
content: Jeevan Pramaan is a biometric-based digital life certificate for pensioners, eliminating the need to visit the bank in person. WHY IT'S NEEDED: All EPS pensioners must submit a life certificate every year to continue receiving pension. Without it, pension is stopped after the deadline month. WHERE TO SUBMIT DIGITAL LIFE CERTIFICATE (DLC): 1. CSC (Common Service Centre) — any nearby CSC with biometric device 2. Pension disbursing bank branch (bring Aadhaar) 3. Post Office / IPPB (India Post Payments Bank) — many branches have biometric device 4. EPFO Regional / Sub-Regional offices 5. UMANG app (if smartphone has biometric/fingerprint reader) 6. Doorstep banking service (SBI, BOB etc. — call your bank) 7. Jeevan Pramaan app — if you have registered fingerprint reader at home WHAT'S NEEDED: Aadhaar number, Aadhaar-registered mobile number (for OTP), pension payment order (PPO) number. WHEN TO SUBMIT: Any time in November. Deadline: 30th November each year (extended sometimes). PHYSICAL LIFE CERTIFICATE (if DLC not possible): Printed life certificate + non-remarriage certificate (for widow pension), attested by Bank Manager or Gazetted Officer. Submit to pension disbursing bank or EPFO office. IF PENSION STOPPED: Contact pension disbursing bank and EPFO. Submit DLC or physical certificate. Resume may take 5-10 working days after submission. CHECKING PENSION STATUS: Login to member portal > Pensioner Portal > View pension payment details.
conditions: IF pensioner == true AND life_cert_not_submitted THEN pension_may_be_stopped

title: Top EPF Claim Rejection Reasons and How to Fix Each (2024)
content: Common EPF claim rejection reasons and resolutions compiled from EPFO field office reports and member portal data. TOP 10 CLAIM REJECTION REASONS: 1. BANK ACCOUNT NOT VERIFIED / IFSC INCORRECT Fix: Re-enter bank details in KYC section. Get current IFSC from bank. Wait for employer approval. 2. DATE OF EXIT NOT UPDATED Fix: If 60+ days since leaving — self-update via Manage > Mark Exit. If less than 60 days — ask employer to update in their portal. 3. NAME MISMATCH (Aadhaar vs PF records) Fix: Submit Joint Declaration for name correction. Employer approves. Then re-link Aadhaar. 4. MEMBER ALREADY SETTLED (Duplicate claim attempt) Fix: Check passbook — if first claim was settled, the amount should be in your bank. If not, file grievance. 5. UAN NOT AADHAAR SEEDED Fix: Link Aadhaar via member portal > Manage > KYC > Aadhaar. Wait for UIDAI verification. 6. CLAIM FOR WRONG PURPOSE (e.g. Form 19 filed while still employed) Fix: Wait 2 months after leaving service, then file again. 7. INSUFFICIENT SERVICE FOR ADVANCE TYPE Fix: Check eligibility table. Select correct advance purpose matching your service period. 8. DOJ (Date of Joining) NOT UPDATED OR INCORRECT Fix: Contact employer to update DOJ via employer portal. Raise JD request if employer is unavailable. 9. PAN NOT LINKED (causes high TDS issue) Fix: Link PAN in member portal > Manage > KYC > PAN. Employer approval needed. 10. ACTIVE CLAIM ALREADY PENDING (Can't file when claim in process) Fix: Wait for current claim to be settled or withdrawn. Track via Online Services > Track Claim Status. GENERAL ADVICE: After any rejection, wait 24 hours before re-filing. Screenshot the rejection reason. If cause is employer-dependent and unresolved after 7 days, file EPFiGMS grievance.
conditions: IF claim_rejected AND reason == bank_not_verified THEN reenter_bank_details
IF claim_rejected AND reason == date_of_exit_missing THEN update_exit_date
IF claim_rejected AND reason == name_mismatch THEN file_joint_declaration

title: EPFO Coverage and Scale — Key Statistics 2024
content: EPFO (Employees' Provident Fund Organisation) is one of the world's largest social security organisations. COVERAGE (2024 estimates): - Active EPF members: ~7 crore (70 million) active contributing members - Total UAN generated: ~32 crore - Annual new payroll additions (new formal sector jobs): ~1.5–2 crore per year - Total PF corpus managed: ~Rs.21 lakh crore+ (one of largest fund pools globally) - Pensioners receiving EPS pension: ~75 lakh (7.5 million) - Active establishments registered: ~7.5 lakh WHO MUST REGISTER UNDER EPF: - Factories with 20+ workers - Establishments in specified industries (list in Schedule I of Act) with 20+ workers - Can voluntarily register with fewer than 20 workers (Section 1(4)) - Once covered, continues even if strength falls below 20 WHO IS EXCLUDED FROM EPF COVERAGE: - Government employees covered by PF under their own service rules - Cooperative societies with <50 employees working without power - Central/State Govt. establishments with their own PF - Apprentices under Apprentices Act 1961 EPF vs NPS (National Pension System) vs PPF (Public Provident Fund): EPF: Mandatory for eligible employees. Both employer + employee contribute. Guaranteed interest. EPFO-managed. NPS: Voluntary/mandatory for Govt employees post-2004. Market-linked. PFRDA-managed. PPF: Voluntary individual account. 15-year lock-in. Tax-free. No employer contribution. Post office / bank-managed. EPF is the only scheme with mandatory employer contribution for private sector employees.
conditions: IF establishment_employees < 20 AND voluntary_option THEN can_register_under_sec_1_4

title: Additional EPFO Data Sources — Collection Guide
content:
This document lists additional official sources to collect for enriching the EPFO Knowledge Base.

1. EPF SCHEME 1952 (Full Text):
URL: https://www.epfindia.gov.in/site_docs/PDFs/Downloads_PDFs/EPFScheme.pdf
Content: Para 60 (interest), Para 68 (advances), Para 69 (withdrawal), Para 70 (settlement on death), Para 72 (payment), Para 83 (IW).
Priority: HIGH — primary source for all withdrawal/advance eligibility conditions.

2. EMPLOYEES' PENSION SCHEME 1995 (Full Text):
URL: https://www.epfindia.gov.in/site_docs/PDFs/Downloads_PDFs/EPSScheme.pdf
Content: Pension formula, family pension, scheme certificate, withdrawal benefit table.
Priority: HIGH — primary source for all EPS-related queries.

3. EDLI SCHEME 1976 (Full Text):
URL: https://www.epfindia.gov.in/site_docs/PDFs/Downloads_PDFs/EDLIScheme.pdf
Content: Insurance benefit calculation, eligibility, payment rules.
Priority: MEDIUM.

4. CIRCULARS — TDS:
URL: https://www.epfindia.gov.in/site_docs/PDFs/Circulars/Y2021-2022/ (GSR 604E dated 31.08.2021)
Content: Taxable contribution rules, TDS rates, split accounts.
Priority: HIGH.

5. HIGHER PENSION CIRCULARS:
URLs:
- Pension/2022/54877/15149 dated 29.12.2022
- Pension/2022/56259/16541 dated 20.02.2023
Content: Joint option verification process, pensionable salary calculation.
Priority: HIGH (active queries).

6. KYC SOPs:
URL: https://www.epfindia.gov.in (search for SOP Joint Declaration)
Content: Documents required for each type of correction.
Priority: HIGH.

7. ABRY SCHEME (ATMANIRBHAR BHARAT):
URL: https://www.epfindia.gov.in/site_en/abry.php
Content: Government subsidy for new employees registered post COVID. Establishments with <1000 employees.
Priority: MEDIUM.

8. MANUAL OF ACCOUNTING PROCEDURE (MAP) Vol. II:
URL: Internal EPFO document
Content: Authorised officials list (Para 10.18) for closed establishment KYC.
Priority: MEDIUM.

9. JEEVAN PRAMAAN / DIGITAL LIFE CERTIFICATE:
URL: https://jeevanpramaan.gov.in
Content: Facial authentication process for pension life certificate.
Priority: MEDIUM.

10. EPFO ANNUAL REPORTS:
URL: https://www.epfindia.gov.in/site_en/Annual_Report.php
Content: Coverage statistics, claim settlement data.
Priority: LOW (analytics only).

11. UMANG APP DOCUMENTATION:
URL: https://web.umang.gov.in/web_new/login
Content: All EPFO services available via UMANG, step-by-step guides.
Priority: MEDIUM.

12. STATE-SPECIFIC CONTACTS:
URL: https://www.epfindia.gov.in/site_en/Contact_us.php
Content: Zonal offices, regional offices, sub-regional offices.
Priority: MEDIUM (for escalation routing).

DATA COLLECTION APPROACH:
1. Use EPFO's official PDF circulars — parse with pdfplumber
2. Scrape EPFO FAQ page at https://www.epfindia.gov.in/site_en/FAQ.php (Bootstrap accordion)
3. Download and parse EPF/EPS/EDLI Scheme text PDFs
4. Manually curate higher-priority circulars (TDS, Higher Pension)
5. Consider using RTI portal responses for edge cases

title: Minimum Wage Schedule 2024: Delhi
content: Under the Minimum Wages Act 1948, the state government of Delhi sets the statutory minimum wage for scheduled employments. As of the 2024 notification, an unskilled worker must receive at least ₹17494 per month. A skilled or highly skilled worker must receive at least ₹21215 per month, excluding discretionary allowances. Paying below these rates violates Section 22 of the Minimum Wages Act and is a criminal offence.

title: Professional Tax: Delhi (No PT Levied)
content: Delhi has not enacted a Professional Tax law. Employers operating in Delhi must not deduct any Professional Tax from employees' salaries. Any such deduction would be unlawful.
